item_0.note: Case: Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank, Knox Circuit Court. Client: Amy Mills. The file note describes a chain of emails in September through October 2025 in which Amy Mills’ counsel sought to retain David Johnson of Johnson Health and Safety as a consulting expert for the matter, with coordination of an upcoming site inspection and related terms.

The initial contact occurred on September 10, 2025, when Adam Redden of DECAMILLIS & MATTINGLY, PLLC, wrote to David Johnson to discuss retaining him as a consulting expert for the case Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank. Adam asked if David had time today or tomorrow for a discussion about the retention. He included his firm’s contact details: DECAMILLIS & MATTINGLY, PLLC, 138 S. 3rd Street, Louisville, Kentucky 40202, with phone [PHONE-1] and fax [PHONE-2], and he also noted at the end that the message contained a confidentiality and privilege notice.

Over the next day, there was ongoing scheduling dialogue. Adam indicated that an afternoon meeting would work for him, and David Johnson proposed a 3:00 PM time in EST for the conversation. There were further exchanges about possible times, with Adam replying that the afternoon would work and David trying to reach him by phone. The thread shows multiple correspondents being cc’d, including Robert Mattingly, Lauren Byrn, Taylor Brown, and Aaron Whaley.

On September 12, 2025, David Johnson wrote again to Adam Redden, noting that he could discuss either that day or the next afternoon and offering a concrete time window. In parallel, David attached a retainer consulting agreement, a CV, a Litigation Summary, and a 2025 Fee Schedule to the thread (the attachment notes indicate that fee information was contained in Section 5). Adam’s responses and the ongoing exchange continued, with references to the confidentiality and privilege notices typical of these communications.

Later in September, there was a discussion about sending and executing the retainer agreement and related case materials. On September 30, 2025, David asked Adam whether the retainer consulting agreement had been executed and whether it had been mailed along with any case information, or if it had already been sent. Adam replied later that day that he planned to send the retainer check and the executed agreement within the week, and he noted that he was waiting on some dates for an inspection.

On October 1, 2025, Adam informed David that he expected to have the retainer check and executed agreement out to him that week. The thread also reiterates the standard confidential notices included in each email.

The matter then moved toward scheduling an inspection. On October 15, 2025, Adam asked David whether he had availability on October 27 or October 29 to perform an inspection at the bank in Corbin, Kentucky, noting that it would be about a three-hour drive for Adam and proposing around 12:00 p.m. ET on either day. David replied the same day that he could do October 29 at 12:30 p.m. EST and requested a confirmation plus a good time to discuss the scope of the inspection beforehand.

On October 16, 2025, Adam confirmed that they were set for October 29 and indicated he was fairly open on October 24, 27, or 28 for a phone call to discuss the scope. David responded that he would attempt to reach Adam in the early afternoon on the 24th and would try again on the 27th if he missed him. Adam followed up on October 17, 2025, providing his cell phone number [PHONE-2980]) in case he could not be reached at the office, and restated his firm’s contact details: DECAMILLIS & MATTINGLY, PLLC, 138 S. 3rd Street, Louisville, KY 40202, with the same phone and fax numbers. The email also reiterates the confidentiality and privilege notices standard to the correspondence.

Throughout the thread, the communications include the usual professional signatures with firm addresses and phone numbers, and each message contains confidentiality notices indicating the information is privileged and intended for the recipient only. There is also mention of scheduled communications and potential in-person inspection logistics related to the Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank matter, including the anticipated inspection in Corbin, Kentucky, and the planned discussion of the scope of that inspection prior to the visit.

item_1.note: On October 23, 2025 at 10:44:26 AM, G. Adam Redden of Decamillis & Mattingly, PLLC sent an email tagged to a project to Lauren regarding Amy Mills. The message stated that Amy had still not responded to any of Redden’s phone calls or emails and requested that a letter be prepared for Amy Mills to be sent via US mail with a self-addressed stamped envelope, concerning Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank.

The proposed letter to Ms. Mills would begin with “Dear Ms. Mills,” and explain that, before any medical providers will talk with them about her injuries and treatment, a signed HIPAA authorization is required. Ms. Mills would be asked to sign the enclosed HIPAA authorization and return it in the enclosed self-addressed stamped envelope. The letter would also request setting up a time to discuss her case, a second mediation, an independent medical examination, and other steps needed to keep the case moving forward. Ms. Mills would be asked to respond to the email chain with some dates and times she would be available to talk. As noted in a recent email, Redden would be in town for an inspection of the bank on Wednesday October 29, 2025, and if she would like to meet before or after that inspection, that would be fantastic. The letter would close with “Cordially by me,” and list enclosures: HIPAA authorization and a self-addressed stamped envelop.

The email included Redden’s contact information: DECAMILLIS & MATTINGLY, PLLC, 138 S. 3rd Street, Louisville, Kentucky 40202; Phone [PHONE-5]; Fax [PHONE-6]. A reference image placeholder, [cid:2a7bceca-d452-4b90-93dc-2992ddec566b], appeared in the message. A confidentiality notice followed, stating that the information in the email and any attached files is confidential and subject to privilege, intended only for the reading and use by the intended recipient; if not the intended recipient, the message and attachments should be deleted and the sender notified to prevent further inadvertent transmissions. Any disclosure, copying, or distribution is strictly prohibited, and recipients are advised to destroy the communication and attachments if they are not the intended recipient.

item_11.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise [DOB-2951]. Client: Amy Mills.

On April 8, 2025, at 14:50:46 UTC, Chelsea Rivera, ROI Quality Analyst at MediCopy, sent a Box file titled AMY MILLS.pdf to Sarena at whaleylawfirm.com. The email indicates the file was delivered with both a “AMY MILLS.pdf” attachment name and a Box access link: https://medicopy.box.com/s/plyd4927var4wii8363pul2hbjyctkhm, along with a separate view URL: https://medicopy.box.com/s/plyd4927var4wii8363pul2hbjyctkhm. The From line identifies Chelsea Rivera- ROI Quality Analyst, with the address [EMAIL-33], and the To line shows sarena@whaleylawfirm.com. The message includes the standard Box footer and prompts for Box actions such as viewing the file and downloading it.

The body of the message states, in all caps: "WE RECEIVED AN UPDATED REQUEST FROM YOU BUT THERE ARE NO NEW RECORDS. THERE ARE JUST THE ONES THAT HAVE BEEN PREVIOUSLY SENT SO I AM RESENDING THIS ONE TO YOU. IF YOU NEED ACTUAL IMAGES THEN YOU WILL NEED TO REACH OUT TO THE FACILITY DIRECTLY. Please click “View File” to access the medical records you have requested. The password for access will be “MCSI” plus the patient's four-digit birth year (i.e. MCSI1912). Please download the file, as the link will disable in 10 days. If you need further assistance, please contact our office at [PHONE-2949]. Thank you." The file is accessible via the link provided above, and there is also a "View File" option.

In addition to the primary links, the message invites viewing on mobile with a Box app link, and provides a Box account footer including the organization address: © 2025 | 900 Jefferson Avenue, Redwood City, CA 94063, USA. There are standard Box options for About Box, Edit Notification Settings, and Privacy Policy, as well as a MediCopy label. The email includes a note to unsubscribe from these emails via a provided link, though the exact unsubscribe URL isn’t shown in the content provided.

item_15.note: In the Mills matter, the file labeled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-51] involves client Amy Mills. The thread titled “Re: FW: Mills,” dated March 21, 2025, shows a series of internal actions and communications among The Whaley Law Firm staff. On March 21, 2025, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager at The Whaley Law Firm, created a Dropbox folder and shared it with DC and the assistant; the assistant acknowledged the link; she drafted a NOS; she replied to the assistant’s acknowledgment with the NOS; and she created a hard CD which FG will mail out with a copy of the NOS and the Certification Pages. Sarena’s contact information is: The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; direct line [PHONE-52]; office line [PHONE-53] ext 104; email Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com (noting the new email address).

The surrounding email activity shows a sequence of communications from mid-February to March 2025 regarding imaging and records. On February 27, 2025, Betsy Catron emailed Aaron Whaley to say, “Betsy, we have sent you everything we have. I will continue to send you anything we get in,” and she added that she would appreciate being kept informed should any surgery be scheduled. Earlier that day, Aaron had replied to Betsy saying that all available materials had been sent and that he would continue to forward anything received. Betsy had previously asked on February 27, 2025, at 3:49 PM to be kept informed about any surgery. On March 12, 2025, at 10:48 AM, Betsy sent a forwarded message asking Aaron where his client had her September 11, 2024 MRI done. Later that same day, at 12:19 PM, Aaron asked, “Do we have this?” and at 1:05 PM, Sarena responded that the results were in the CHI St. Joseph Orthopedics Records (Pages 25–30), appeared completed in-house, and that the results were attached. The thread shows that on March 14, 2025, at 3:49 PM, Aaron requested the actual films for all MRI/CT/X-ray studies. On March 17, 2025, at 3:25 PM, Sarena wrote that she had called Radiology, obtained a direct fax number, and sent the CRR. On March 20, 2025, at 11:55 AM, Sarena noted that she received an imaging CD and had placed it in Aaron’s chair; later, at 2:01 PM, Aaron asked if a copy could be made and forwarded to defense counsel. On March 21, 2025, Sarena finalized the earlier steps by confirming the Dropbox folder creation, sharing, and related administrative tasks (as described above), including drafting and signing off on the NOS and arranging a hard CD distribution.

All individuals referenced in the thread and the key participants include Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, and Aaron Whaley. Their communications originate from The Whaley Law Firm’s Louisville, Kentucky office at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with the contact details cited above for Sarena. The chain also includes Betsy Catron, who appears to be communicating with Aaron regarding MRI history and potential surgery timing. The thread confirms the following documented actions: creation of a Dropbox folder and sharing with the defense and assistant; acknowledgment of the shared link; drafting of the NOS; correspondence acknowledging the NOS; creation of a hard CD containing the NOS and Certification Pages; and ongoing handling of imaging CDs, radiology communications, and requests for actual film copies, as well as the location of prior MRI work and related records.

item_17.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise – [DOB-2922]. Client: Amy Mills.

On March 21, 2025, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email regarding the subject “Re: Sarena Tuttle shared '2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC' with you.” In that message, she thanked the recipient for acknowledging receipt and stated that the Notice of Service would be filed momentarily. She also noted that a hard CD was en route via USPS. Her contact details appeared in the signature: The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, D: [PHONE-2921], O: [PHONE-2920] ext 104, E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com (PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS).

The email was sent to Elizabeth Romersa at Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, CC’ing Aaron Whaley, with the message header “Re: Sarena Tuttle shared '2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC' with you.” A prior thread excerpt shows the message chain including a note that a hard CD was being mailed and a brief acknowledgment of receipt.

Earlier that same morning, at 9:41 AM, Sarena Tuttle sent an email to Elizabeth Romersa via Dropbox with the subject “Sarena Tuttle shared '2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC' with you.” The body stated that this email contains a link that cannot be inspected for phishing or viruses and advised verifying the email’s authenticity with the sender using the organization’s trusted contact list before replying or taking further action. The Dropbox message noted that Sarena Tuttle invited Elizabeth to view the folder named "*2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC*" on Dropbox, and included the message: “Please find attached Ms. Mills Imaging CD contents from CHI St. Joseph Orthopedics - Dr. Kevin Magone, MD.” The email included options such as “Add to Dropbox,” and text inviting the recipient to use Dropbox’s mobile or desktop apps. It also included the standard prompt “Is there an issue? Report to Dropbox.”

Elizabeth Romersa’s reply on March 21, 2025, around 10:32 AM, simply stated: “Thank you.” Her signature identifies her as a Paralegal to Walter Ward, William Partin, Betsy Catron, James Freel, Whitney Williams, and Jeanette Jayne at Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507. Her phone number is [PHONE-2919], fax [PHONE-65], and her website is www.whtlaw.com. A confidentiality notice followed: “Information contained in this email transmission is privileged and confidential. If you are not the intended recipient, do not read, distribute or reproduce this transmission (including any attachments). If you have received this electronic mail transmission in error, please delete it from your system without copying it, and notify the sender by reply email, so that our records can be corrected. Thank you.”

item_18.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise file, dated [DOB-2915]. Client: Amy Mills.

On March 21, 2025, Elizabeth Romersa, a paralegal at Vine Center/Whit Law (counsel for a list of clients including Walter Ward, William Partin, Betsy Catron, James Freel, Whitney Williams, and Jeanette Jayne), sent an email to Sarena Tuttle of Whaley Law Firm with the subject “RE: Sarena Tuttle shared ‘2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC’ with you.” The message, dated Friday, March 21, 2025 at 14:32:04 +0000, simply said “Thank you.” Elizabeth’s signature followed, identifying her as Paralegal to the aforementioned attorneys, and including the Vine Center contact information:
Vine Center
333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100
Lexington, KY 40507
P [PHONE-69]
F [PHONE-70]
www.whtlaw.com

The email also carried a standard privileged and confidential notice: Information contained in this email transmission is privileged and confidential. If you are not the intended recipient, do not read, distribute or reproduce this transmission (including any attachments). If you have received this electronic mail transmission in error, please delete it from your system without copying it, and notify the sender by reply email, so that our records can be corrected. Thank you.

Earlier in the thread, on the same date, Sarena Tuttle (via Dropbox) sent an email to Elizabeth Romersa with the subject “Sarena Tuttle shared ‘2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC’ with you.” The message, timestamped Friday, March 21, 2025 at 9:41 AM, began with the notice that the email contains a link that cannot be inspected for phishing or viruses, advising the recipient to verify the email’s authenticity with the sender using the organization’s trusted contact list before replying or taking further action. The message included a Dropbox invitation:
Sarena Tuttle (sarena@whaleylawfirm.com) invited you to view the folder “2023.03.21 Mills Imaging CD to DC” on Dropbox. It stated, “Please find attached Ms. Mills Imaging CD contents from CHI St. Joseph Orthopedics - Dr. Kevin Magone, MD.” The email also offered actions such as “Add to Dropbox” and included prompts to Do more with your files—get the desktop and mobile apps, along with links and icons for Dropbox for Mobile and Dropbox for Desktop. A closing line asked, “Is there an issue? Report to Dropbox.”

item_19.note: This case note concerns the Mills matter for client Amy Mills, filed as Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2910]. The correspondence span from February 27, 2025 to March 20, 2025 and centers on imaging records, radiology information, and communications with Betsy Catron, Aaron Whaley, and Sarena Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm.

On March 20, 2025, at 2:01 AM Eastern Time, Aaron Whaley sent an email to Sarena Tuttle and the Amy Mills Premise file (Amy Mills Premise [DOB-74]) asking, “Can you make a copy and forward to defense counsel? Thanks.” Later that same day, at 11:55 AM (the record notes a question mark after the time), Sarena Tuttle replied: “Received Imaging CD - Placed in your chair,” and she included her standard contact information as Litigation Paralegal & Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm.

In the earlier portion of the chain, on March 17, 2025 at 3:25 PM, Sarena wrote that she had called Radiology, obtained a direct fax number, and sent the CRR. Then, on March 14, 2025, Aaron Whaley had requested the actual films for all of Amy Mills's MRI, CT, and X-ray studies.

On March 12, 2025, at 1:05 PM, Sarena informed the team that the results were in the CHI St. Joseph Orthopedics Records, spanning pages 25–30, appearing to be completed in-house, and that the results were attached. Earlier that same day at 12:19 PM, Aaron Whaley asked, “Do we have this?” In relation to that inquiry, a forwarded message from Betsy Catron appeared, dated March 12, 2025 at 10:48 AM, with the subject “FW: Mills.” Betsy asked Aaron: “Hi Aaron – can you tell me where your client had her Sept 11, 2024 MRI done?”

The chain also includes Betsy’s prior correspondence from February 27, 2025. In Betsy’s 3:49 PM message to Aaron, she wrote: “I appreciate it, Aaron. Please keep me informed should any surgery be scheduled.” Aaron replied to Betsy on February 27, 2025 at 1:33 PM: “Betsy, we have sent you everything we have. I will continue to send you anything we get in.” This message carried the standard external email caution notice indicating it was sent from outside the recipient’s organization, advising caution with links and attachments. The communications included the sign-offs from Aaron Whaley and the Whaley Law Firm, listing their address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, and the corresponding phone numbers and email addresses, with Aaron Whaley repeatedly signing as the author and Sarena Tuttle providing her role and contact information.

In sum, the working thread shows coordination to locate and forward Amy Mills’s imaging records, including an imaging CD that was received and placed for access, requests for the actual films of MRI/CT/X-ray studies, and inquiries about the location of a September 11, 2024 MRI, with updates about CHI St. Joseph Orthopedics records (Pages 25–30) and confirmation that results were attached. The primary objective stated in the March 20 message was to copy and forward the materials to defense counsel.

item_24.note: Amy Mills is the client in the case titled Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank, with the internal file name Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2888]. On February 27, 2025, the matter was referenced in an email chain labeled Re: Amy Mills v Forcht Bank - PL Supp Resp to DC Forcht Disc Req. The email thread involved Sarena Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, and Aaron Whaley, with Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2887] CC’d on the exchanges.

In an email sent on the morning of February 27, 2025, at 9:44 AM, Sarena Tuttle wrote to Mrs. Catron that Mrs. Mills’s Supplemental Responses to the Defendant’s 3rd and 4th Discovery Requests were attached, and she invited any questions or discussions to address the matter in further detail. The message included Sarena’s contact information and noted that there was a new email address for her: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com. Her signature identified her as the Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager for The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with D: [PHONE-2886], O: [PHONE-2885] ext 104.

Earlier in the day, at 1:22 PM, Aaron Whaley replied, noting, “Make sure the actual records and bills are attached to them that we have received.” His signature followed, showing his affiliation with The Whaley Law Firm and listing the firm’s address—712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222—and the contact number [PHONE-2884].

Later still, at 1:27 PM, Sarena responded with the line, “The packet was 72 pages, I assume all pages were attached correctly.” This message carried her standard signature block, reiterating her role at The Whaley Law Firm and including the firm’s address, direct line (D: [PHONE-2883]), main line (O: [PHONE-2882] ext 104), and her email address Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, again noting the parenthetical reminder to please note the new email address. The message also reflected the same case context and the subject matter regarding supplemental discovery responses.

The email chain also shows that the message to Mrs. Catron included the attachment of Mrs. Mills’s Supplemental Responses to the Defendant’s 3rd and 4th Discovery Requests, with Sarena’s note that if there were questions, they should be discussed directly. The CC for the correspondence included Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2881] at the email [EMAIL-103].

For completeness, Aaron Whaley’s signature appeared with the Whaley Law Firm details: 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, [PHONE-2879], following his reply.

item_29.note: The case is Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-128], involving client Amy Mills. On February 26, 2025, at 03:33:14 GMT-05:00, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email addressed to Aaron Whaley with the subject “Re: response to IROG.” The message was also cc’d to the Amy Mills Premise [DOB-129] Filevine project email address. In the body, Sarena apologized for the delayed production and advised that the attached documents were provided for Aaron’s approval prior to filing that day. She noted that the attachments include Dr. Magone and PT Pro CMR/CMBs.

Sarena’s contact details followed in the signature: she is a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her direct line is [PHONE-130], and the office line is [PHONE-2852] ext 104. Her email is Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note indicating this is her new email address.

The message thread also contains a prior email from Aaron Whaley, dated Friday, February 21, 2025, at 1:12 PM, in which he wrote: “I know we responded to the set they sent us in August but that was before we got in the new medical records. I know you have been serving the medical records to them but we need to amend the answers to IROG with the new records before the 45 days is up which is the end of next week. I would like to get that filed no later than wednesday Feb. 26.” Aaron Whaley’s signature follows, identifying him with The Whaley Law Firm and listing the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, and phone number [PHONE-2851].

item_30.note: On February 24, 2025 at 1:04 PM (UTC-05:00), Betsy and Aaron spoke with [EMAIL-135] about Amy Mills’s case. The conversation centered on the client’s ongoing medical issues and the difficulties of negotiating a settlement due to her persistent pain and upcoming surgery.

Aaron and Betsy discussed the client’s situation and potential settlement negotiations. Aaron informed Betsy that the client had a surgery scheduled for January 3, which was canceled because of bad weather, and that she has been unwell recently. The client remains in pain and has not been able to engage in settlement discussions. Aaron noted that the client is unlikely to lower her settlement demand of $1.5 million in light of her ongoing pain and medical issues. Betsy suggested obtaining the most recent medical records to assess the situation and track expenses. They agreed to stay updated on the client’s surgery timeline to better understand the progression of the case.

Next steps: Aaron should send the most recent medical records to Betsy this week and keep Betsy informed about the client’s surgery schedule as it develops. The note is signed, Best, Aaron Whaley.

item_32.note: On February 3, 2025, the Boyd Circuit Court entered an order in the above-styled case Cordle, Charles v. Walter, Casey et al., Case No. 22-CI-00747. The notice of entry was transmitted electronically from [EMAIL-143] and constitutes service under Kentucky Rule of Civil Procedure 77.04. The header shows the notice date as February 3, 2025 at 09:45:38 -0500, and the documented processing time is February 3, 2025 at 9:43 a.m. Eastern. The envelope number associated with this filing is 9796923, and the case caption is Cordle, Charles v. Walter, Casey et al.

The notice indicates that an order has been entered in the case and that the document included in this eFiling is an ORDER SCHEDULING. Yocum ([EMAIL-145]), Langdon Worley ([EMAIL-146]), and Joshua J. Owen ([EMAIL-147]).

On Wednesday, January 29, 2025, at 14:31:46 UTC, Chelsea Rivera, ROI Quality Analyst, sent a file titled "AMY MILLS (NR).pdf" on Box to sarena@whaleylawfirm.com. The From address on the message is Chelsea Rivera- ROI Quality Analyst ([EMAIL-2832]) and the To address is sarena@whaleylawfirm.com. The password for access will be “MCSI” plus the patient's four-digit birth year (i.e. MCSI1912). Please download the file, as the link will disable in 10 days. If you need further assistance, please contact our office at [PHONE-2831]. Thank you." The view file link is repeated as well as a prompt to view the file, along with a prompt to download since the link will expire in 10 days. For assistance, the box office number provided is [PHONE-2830].

The email also includes a prompt to Get our app to view this on mobile, with a mobile app link, and additional Box links for viewing on mobile or other access options, including "View File" and various app-related links. The message footer lists Box-related information including the corporate address: © 2025 | 900 Jefferson Avenue, Redwood City, CA 94063, USA, and provides About Box, Edit Notification Settings, and Privacy Policy links, along with a note about Box access and settings.

The message is signed with "MediCopy," and there is a line noting: "To unsubscribe from these emails please click the following link:" though the actual unsubscribe URL is not shown in the provided text.

On January 29, 2025, The Whaley Law Firm’s Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager, sent an email to Chelsea Rivera, a ROI Quality Analyst at MediCopy. The message acknowledged receipt of Chelsea’s response to a request for certified medical records and billing for the firm’s client, Amy Mills, from CHI St. Joseph Medical Group – Radiology. In Chelsea’s reply, she stated there were no “new” dates of service. Sarena noted that Amy Mills did receive treatment on various dates between *[DOB-2827] & [DOB-2826]* and requested that MediCopy provide any and all records requested, not limited to new dates of service, so that a complete copy covering the period listed could be supplied. Sarena’s contact details are listed as D: [PHONE-158], O: [PHONE-159] ext 104, E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. The message also includes the parenthetical note: (PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS).

The email thread also contains a forwarded message from Chelsea Rivera. Dated Wednesday, January 29, 2025 at 9:31 AM, the subject line reads: “Chelsea Rivera- ROI Quality Analyst sent you a file 'AMY MILLS (NR).pdf' on Box.” The forwarded note indicates that Chelsea Rivera- ROI Quality Analyst ([EMAIL-160]) has sent a file named AMY MILLS (NR).pdf. The recipient is instructed to click “View File” to access the requested medical records. The password for access will be “MCSI” plus the patient’s four-digit birth year (for example, MCSI1912). The message warns to download the file because the link will disable in 10 days, and provides a contact number for assistance: [PHONE-161]. The footer and closing lines include View File, a prompt to get the MediCopy app to view on mobile, and the 2025 Box copyright notice: © 2025 | 900 Jefferson Avenue, Redwood City, CA 94063, USA. It also lists Box’s About, Notification Settings, Privacy Policy links and a note about unsubscribing from MediCopy emails.

On Monday, January 27, 2025, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager for The Whaley Law Firm, sent the Updated PT Pro Records & Bills requested in the Amy Mills file. The email stated, “Here are the Updated PT Pro Records & Bills you requested. Delivery via USPS today.” The signature block for this message listed Sarena M. Tuttle’s contact information at The Whaley Law Firm: 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; D: [PHONE-170]; O: [PHONE-171] ext 104; E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note: “PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.”

In response, at approximately 11:41 AM on the same day, Aaron Whaley replied: “thanks, we need the radiology records that are mentioned in here.”

Earlier still, at approximately 9:49 AM on January 21, 2025, Sarena had written: “Hey AW, Here is Amy's updated Ortho CMR & CMB you requested.” Her signature followed, again listing The Whaley Law Firm’s address and contact information, and noting the new email address.

The thread concludes with the closing signature blocks for both Sarena Tuttle and Aaron Whaley. Aaron Whaley’s signature appears as: Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, [PHONE-2808].

item_38.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-177]. Client: Amy Mills. Reviewing activity in Amy Mills Premise [DOB-178] for the period from December 25, 2024, through January 24, 2025, the case involves Amy Mills suing Forcht Bank, likely for a fall incident that occurred in 2019. The plaintiff has ongoing medical issues and treatments related to the incident, including shoulder problems that require surgery.

The most recent activity shows that the defendant filed a Motion for Contempt and to Impose Sanctions on January 7, 2025, with a hearing scheduled for January 13, 2025.

A potential red flag is that the plaintiff's discovery responses were deemed deficient by the defense, particularly regarding the disclosure of new medical providers and upcoming surgery information. This deficiency contributed to the Motion for Contempt.

The next steps should include gathering and providing updated medical information, specifically about Dr. Kevin Magone in London, KY, who is treating the plaintiff, and details about the postponed January 3rd surgery. This information should be promptly shared with the defense to address the discovery issues.

item_48.note: On January 15, 2025, the Knox Circuit Court in Kentucky issued a Notice of Entry in the case Mills, Amy v. Forcht Bank, Case Number 20-CI-00112, with Envelope Number 9652448. The notice originated from [EMAIL-227] and was directed to the Whaley Law Firm at addresses including [EMAIL-228], Sarena@whaleylawfirm.com, and [EMAIL-229]. The To line also lists recipients by email such as [EMAIL-230], Sarena@whaleylawfirm.com, and [EMAIL-2752], although the notice itself indicates electronic mailing to four recipients: Aaron Gregory Whaley ([EMAIL-2751]), Gregg E. Thornton ([EMAIL-2750]), Betsy R. Catron ([EMAIL-2749]), and Joshua Ellis ([EMAIL-235]). The case caption is Mills, Amy v. Forcht Bank, and the envelope number associated with this notice is 9652448. The notice was sent on behalf of the Knox Circuit Court.

The notice states that orders have been entered in the above-styled case, and that electronic transmission of the notice constitutes service in accordance with CR 77.04. The notice was processed on January 15, 2025, at 8:01 a.m. Eastern Time, and the email transmission reflecting the notice was sent at 8:03:03 a.m. (Eastern time, as indicated by the -0500 offset).

The document included with this eFiling is labeled “ORDER - OTHER.” The notice provides a link to view the document: https://kcoj.kycourts.net/eFilingRetrieval/Home/Package?id=4E593BDA-2B23-449C-8FB0-23CD47979A62.

This notice was automatically generated by the Kentucky Court of Justice eFiling system, and recipients are instructed not to reply to the email. The message also includes the Kentucky Court of Justice Confidentiality Notice, explaining that the message and any attachments are intended only for the addressee and may contain privileged, confidential, and/or proprietary information. If you are not the intended recipient, or an authorized employee, agent, or representative of the intended recipient, you should not read, copy, retain, or disseminate the message or attachments, and you should not forward them without express written consent of the sender. If you have received the message in error, you are asked to contact the sender immediately and delete all copies of the message and any attachments. Transmission or misdelivery does not waive any applicable legal privilege.

Catron ([EMAIL-250]), and Joshua Ellis ([EMAIL-251]). The document can be viewed at the provided URL: https://kcoj.kycourts.net/eFilingRetrieval/Home/Package?id=BB0406DE-F5B9-4CE5-834A-23B795A8AB97. The message includes the Kentucky Court of Justice Confidentiality Notice, which states that the email and any attachments are intended only for the addressee and may contain privileged, confidential, and/or proprietary information; if you are not the intended recipient or authorized representative, you should not read, copy, retain, or disseminate the message or attachments, should not forward them without express written consent, and you should contact the sender and delete all copies if received in error. Transmission or misdelivery does not waive any applicable legal privilege.

item_54.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise, file name Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2720]. The client is Amy Mills.

Re: Record requests. On January 13, 2025, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager for The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email regarding record requests. The message was addressed to Aaron Whaley and CC’d to the Filevine project email for Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2719]. The subject indicates a focus on record requests. In the body of the note, Tuttle records that a DC authentication was sent to the client via DocuSign, that a request for records (CRR) was sent to Dr. Magone via fax (covering 2014 to present), and that a CRR was sent to PT Pros London via email (covering 2014 to present, all locations). Tuttle’s signature block identifies her as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with the firm’s Louisville address, phone numbers, and her email, noting in parentheses to please note the new email address.

That same day, in a reply dated January 13, 2025, at about 9:30 a.m., Aaron Whaley wrote back. He stated that the client is treating with Dr. Magone in London, Kentucky, who is an orthopedic doctor, and that the client is also treated with PT Pros in London. He requested all records from Dr. Magone for the last five years. He believed they had previously requested records from PT Pros in Corbin, so he asked for records from PT Pros London that extend from the earliest date they have on file from that provider. He also requested the bills. He emphasized urgency, noting that the court gave them 45 days to obtain the records and to provide them in their answers to interrogatories. He mentioned that last month they listed some treatment dates, but did not provide any records or bills. He directed that the records and bills be gathered and provided, and that a supplement be prepared for defense counsel. He also asked defense counsel again for the medical authorization to give to the client for the client to sign.

Aaron Whaley’s signature block follows, with his contact information for Whaley Law Firm at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, including the phone numbers [PHONE-2718] and [PHONE-2717].

Note: Sarena M. Tuttle’s signature line includes the same Whaley Law Firm contact details and a parenthetical reminder: PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.

item_55.note: This case note concerns Amy Mills in the matter titled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-268]. The client is Amy Mills, born on [DOB-2982]. The note is a Certified Medical Records & Billing Request for Amy Mills.

The request originated from Sarena Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm. Her email address is sarena@whaleylawfirm.com, and the message was sent to [EMAIL-269] with Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-270]) copied. The email was sent on Monday, January 13, 2025 at 09:57:52 -0500. The header indicates the subject as Certified Medical Records & Billing Request - Amy Mills DOB [DOB-2981].

In the body of the message, Sarena writes: “Good Morning, Please find attached our office's request for certified medical records and medical billing for the above referenced client/patient. Please provide us with copies of any and all records/bills for any and all PT Pro locations. We would greatly appreciate your immediate attention to this matter as the court is awaiting these records and bills. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us directly to discuss them in further detail.”

The message is signed by Sarena M. Tuttle, who identifies herself as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm. The firm’s address is 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her direct line is [PHONE-271], and the office line is [PHONE-272] ext 104. Her email is Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note indicating “PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.”

item_56.note: On January 13, 2025 at 9:30 a.m. Eastern Time, Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm sent an email regarding the case labeled Amy Mills Premise [DOB-274]. The message was sent from [EMAIL-275] to Sarena Tuttle at sarena@whaleylawfirm.com and to the Filevine project address for Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2707], [EMAIL-277]. The client, Amy Mills, is currently treating with Dr. Magone, an orthopedic doctor in London, Kentucky, and has also been treated with PT Pros in London. The email requests all medical records from Dr. Magone for the last five years. It notes that records from PT Pros in Corbin have likely been requested before, and it asks for records from PT Pros dating back to the earliest date available after our last date on file with them. The email also requests all bills related to the treatment and emphasizes that this should be done ASAP.

The sender states that the court has given a 45-day deadline to obtain these records and to provide them in our answers to interrogatories. Last month we listed some treatment dates, but we do not believe we provided any records or bills. The email directs that the records and bills be gathered and provided, and it asks for a supplement to defense counsel. It also asks defense counsel again for the medical authorization that we can give to the client for signing.

Contact information included in the note: Aaron Whaley, Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, phone [PHONE-278]. Email: [EMAIL-279]. The email was sent to sarena@whaleylawfirm.com and to Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2703]’s Filevine project email address [EMAIL-281].

item_58.note: Case: Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank. The file is labeled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2696], and the client is Amy Mills.

In the case caption, the matter is identified as Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank, and the note concerns Plaintiff’s Response to the Defendant’s Discovery Requests (3rd set of Interrogatories and 4th set of Requests for Production of Documents). The communication thread includes messages from The Whaley Law Firm and opposing counsel, with the date of the latest exchange noted as January 12, 2025.

On December 26, 2024, Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email to counsel for the defendant, Betsy Catron, and Gregg Thornton, with copies to Aaron Whaley, Bryce Koon, and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-288]. The subject was “Amy Mills v Forcht Bank - PL Resp to DC Disc Req 3rd & 4th.” The email stated that the plaintiff’s response to the defendant’s third set of Interrogatories and fourth set of Requests for Production of Documents had been attached, and that their Notice of Service had been filed moments earlier via CourtNet Envelope 9457257. The message also included an external email banner warning and provided contact information for Sarena: D: [PHONE-289], O: [PHONE-290] ext. 104, E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, as well as the firm’s Louisville address and related details.

The following day, December 27, 2024, Betsy R. Catron, attorney at Vine Center in Lexington, Kentucky, replied that Aaron Whaley’s responses were deficient. She stated that there were no new medical providers identified and that the record did not indicate who Amy Mills had been treating with over the past year or who would be performing her upcoming surgery. She added that they are not able to subpoena records if they do not know who Amy Mills is treating with, and that if she is alleging that the upcoming surgery is related to her 2019 fall, they are entitled to this information.

On January 12, 2025, at 10:06:41 AM Eastern Time, Aaron Whaley of The Whaley Law Firm sent a follow-up to Betsy Catron. In his message, he noted that Amy Mills is seeing Dr. Kevin Magone in London, Kentucky, who is described as an orthopedic and sports medicine surgeon. He also stated that she had physical therapy at PT Pros in London. He reported that her January 3 surgery had been postponed due to some supply issue, and he concluded with, “Not sure.” The Whaley Law Firm contact details appear in the message: 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, phone [PHONE-291].

On 2025-01-10, an inbound text message was received in relation to the Amy Mills case, coming from [PHONE-316] to [PHONE-2666]. The message content reads as follows: "They can't exclude anything like that when the specialist has clearly linked it to the accident" and from now on I'm not going to include any treatment I'm having until AfTER it is completed 
I'm still not done with the surgery to fix my shoulder and if he refers me out bc of this I'm going to be highly pissed ?? 
Non of this needs to be sent to a specialist either to fork out more money on someone's opinion that probably isn't even as qualified as the physician I am seeing. 

What also needs to be used it the income I was making at the time of the accident! 
I can't use income made the year prior for reason of not making that during the time of the accident which means the amount made at the time of the accident would be the amount calculated on! 
It's not logical to even make that argument of not being able to claim the earnings made for 2019 Jan - April (which taxes were filed) and bc they don't like the number then say, "well there isn't an guarantee you would make that again and

Date and Time: 2025-01-10
From Number: [PHONE-2665]
To Number: [PHONE-319]

This message and/or attachment is intended only for the addressee and may contain information that is privileged, confidential and/or proprietary work product. Forcht Bank, Case Number 20-CI-00112, Envelope Number 9594114. The notice identifies Betsy R. Catron, attorney for the defendant, as the eFiler. The filing timestamp appears in the notice as January 07, 2025 at 12:13 PM Eastern, and the notice was sent from [EMAIL-357].

The notice was electronically mailed to the following recipients: Aaron Gregory Whaley ([EMAIL-2625]), Gregg E. Thornton ([EMAIL-2624]), Betsy R. Catron ([EMAIL-2623]), and Joshua Ellis ([EMAIL-361]). The documents accompanying the eFiling are: Motion for Contempt and to Impose Sanctions, along with Exhibits 1 through 4. The notice provides a link to view the documents: https://kcoj.kycourts.net/eFilingRetrieval/Home/Package?id=2E29F9FF-8613-4BF2-902D-972715B8FFB7.

This message was automatically generated by the Kentucky Court of Justice eFiling system. Do not reply to this email.

item_74.note: Case: Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank – Plaintiff’s Response to Defense Discovery Requests (Third Interrogatories and Fourth Requests for Production)

The matter involves Amy Mills as the client in the case titled Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank, with the file labeled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-363]. On December 26, 2024, Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email to counsel Gregg Thornton and Betsy Catron, with Aaron Whaley, Bryce Koon, and the file “Amy Mills Premise [DOB-364]” cc’d. The subject of the message was “Amy Mills v Forcht Bank - PL Resp to DC Disc Req 3rd & 4th.” In the body of the email, Sarena stated: “Good afternoon Counsel, We hope that you all had a wonderful holiday. Please find attached the Plaintiff's Response to the Defendant Forcht Bank's 3rd set of Interrogatories and 4th set of Requests for Production of Documents. Our Notice of Service was filed moments ago via CourtNet Envelope 9457257. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us directly to discuss them in further detail.” The email noted it was an external email and provided the Whaley Law Firm contact details: The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with D: [PHONE-2618], O: [PHONE-366] ext 104, and the sender’s email address Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com.

On December 27, 2024, Betsy R. Catron, Attorney with Vine Center in Lexington, Kentucky, responded to Aaron Whaley. Her message criticized the plaintiff’s responses as deficient, specifically noting that there were no new medical providers identified—namely, who Amy Mills has been treating with over the past year and who will perform her upcoming surgery. She stated that subpoenas cannot be issued for records if the treating providers are unknown, and she asserted that if the upcoming surgery is claimed to be related to Amy Mills’s 2019 fall, the information about treating providers is entitled to disclosure. She signed with the firm’s details: Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507; P [PHONE-367]; F [PHONE-2615]; www.whtlaw.com.

On December 28, 2024, Aaron Whaley of The Whaley Law Firm replied to Betsy Catron. He wrote, “Betsy I am out of town. I’ll be back Monday January 6. I’ll take a look and update if necessary. Thanks.” His signature followed, listing The Whaley Law Firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with phone numbers [PHONE-369] and [PHONE-370], and noting his email contact as [EMAIL-2612].

item_75.note: Case: Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank; Plaintiff’s Response to Defendant’s Third Set of Interrogatories and Fourth Set of Requests for Production of Documents (PL Resp to DC Disc Req 3rd & 4th). Client: Amy Mills.

On December 26, 2024, Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email to Gregg Thornton and Betsy Catron, with Aaron Whaley, Bryce Koon, and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2610] cc’d. The subject line read: Amy Mills v Forcht Bank - PL Resp to DC Disc Req 3rd & 4th. In the body, Sarena advised that the Plaintiff’s Response to the Defendant Forcht Bank’s 3rd set of Interrogatories and 4th set of Requests for Production of Documents was attached, and that Notice of Service had been filed moments earlier via CourtNet Envelope 9457257. She noted that this message was an external email and included the standard caution about external emails. The contact details listed for The Whaley Law Firm were provided, including the firm’s Louisville address and direct lines, and the email was signed by Sarena M. Tuttle with her updated email address: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com.

The following day, December 27, 2024, Betsy R. Catron, Attorney at Vine Center in Lexington, KY, replied to Aaron Whaley and others. Her message criticized the responses as deficient, stating that there were no new medical providers identified and that the client’s ongoing treatment over the past year, as well as the physician performing her upcoming surgery, had not been disclosed. She explained that they cannot subpoena records without knowing who the client is treating with, and if the upcoming surgery is claimed to be related to the 2019 fall, they are entitled to this information. Betsy signed the email with her attorney credentials and provided her firm’s contact information, including the Vine Center address, phone and fax numbers, and website.

The next day, December 28, 2024, at approximately 5:15 a.m. (the Date stamp on the message being Sat Dec 28, 2024 05:15:31 GMT-05:00), Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm responded from out of town. He wrote that he would be back on Monday, January 6, and that he would review the matter and update if necessary. He concluded with thanks and provided his firm’s contact information, including the Louisville office address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, and his primary phone number, [PHONE-374].

The Whaley Law Firm’s communications were as described, with Sarena Tuttle’s December 26 email including a detailed note about the attached Plaintiff’s Response and the CourtNet filing, Betsy Catron’s December 27 response detailing deficiencies in the production and pursuit of new medical provider information, and Aaron Whaley’s December 28 follow-up indicating he would review the file after returning from being out of town.

item_76.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2607]. The client is Amy Mills. This note concerns the matter captioned Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank and specifically the Plaintiff’s response to the Defendant’s discovery requests, namely the third set of interrogatories and the fourth set of requests for production of documents.

On December 26, 2024, Sarena M. Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm sent an email to Gregg Thornton and Betsy Catron, with the matter titled “Amy Mills v Forcht Bank - PL Resp to DC Disc Req 3rd & 4th.” The email was cc’ed to Aaron Whaley, Bryce Koon, and the file labeled “Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2606].” It is noted as an external email. In the body, Sarena wrote: “Good afternoon Counsel, We hope that you all had a wonderful holiday. Please find attached the Plaintiff's Response to the Defendant Forcht Bank's 3rd set of Interrogatories and 4th set of Requests for Production of Documents. Our Notice of Service was filed moments ago via CourtNet Envelope 9457257. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us directly to discuss them in further detail.” Sarena signed as Litigation Paralegal & Workers’ Compensation Case Manager for The Whaley Law Firm, providing the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with direct line [PHONE-378], office line [PHONE-379] ext 104, and the note “PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.” The signature also includes her email address Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com.

The following day, December 27, 2024, Betsy R. Catron, Attorney at WHT Law, sent a reply to Aaron Whaley, with copies to Sarena Tuttle and Gregg Thornton, and cc’ed the Amy Mills Premise [DOB-380] file. In the body of Betsy’s message, she stated that Aaron’s responses are deficient. She noted there are no new medical providers identified, including who Amy Mills has been treating with throughout the past year and who is performing her upcoming surgery. Betsy explained that they cannot subpoena records if they do not know who Amy Mills has been treating with. She added that if Amy Mills is claiming this upcoming surgery as related to her 2019 fall, they are entitled to this information. Betsy signed as Attorney for WHT Law, and provided her firm’s contact details and location at Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, with phone number [PHONE-381], fax [PHONE-382], and the firm’s website www.whtlaw.com.

In summary, the case note documents two sequential communications regarding the Plaintiff’s Response to the Defendant’s third set of interrogatories and fourth set of requests for production of documents, including notice of service filed via CourtNet envelope 9457257, and a subsequent attorney-to-attorney exchange critiquing the sufficiency of the medical-provider information and the related subpoena needs for records concerning Amy Mills’ medical treatment and upcoming surgery related to the 2019 fall.

item_83.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2563]. Client: Amy Mills. The matter under discussion was the August interrogatories.

On December 23, 2024, in a message titled “Re: August interrogatories,” Sarena M. Tuttle, the Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email from sarena@whaleylawfirm.com to Aaron Whaley at [EMAIL-421]. The message CC’d Amy Mills Premise [DOB-422] – Filevine Project Email Address ([EMAIL-423]). The header showed the date as Mon Dec 23 2024 10:20:34 GMT-05:00, and the body simply stated, “Will do.” Her signature block followed, detailing her position, The Whaley Law Firm’s address (712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222), and contact numbers (D: [PHONE-2559], O: [PHONE-2558] ext 104), along with her new email address (Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com).

Earlier in the thread, there were several back-and-forth exchanges. On December 20, 2024, at about 3:59 PM (timestamp shown as 3:59?PM), Aaron Whaley replied, “Ok, then good to go. File them on December 26.” Before that, at 12:05 PM (timestamp shown as 12:05?PM), Sarena Tuttle explained that a formatting error had occurred with a document she opened on her computer and that she would verify proper formatting on the PDF version upon approval. She signed again with her Whaley Law Firm credentials and new email address.

On December 20, 2024 at about 11:58 AM, Aaron commented that in the provider list, item a) looked blank and suggested it could be a formatting issue. The previous message from Sarena, at 12:24 PM, had provided the draft: “Here you go. :) I listed the known providers for 2023 per the records we have. I also included DOS which I do not normally do. Let me know if there are any changes needed.” This message included Sarena’s full signature block.

On December 17, 2024, at 12:21 PM, Sarena stated: “It is every provider listed in her file, most of which our most recent CMRs are from 2023 through March 2024. However, that is not to say she saw the providers in 2023/2024. Would you like me to review her records & narrow it down?” Aaron replied at 12:12 PM: “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-426]?” Earlier, at 12:38 PM, Aaron requested confirmation, and Sarena affirmed with her signature.

The thread began on December 16, 2024, with Aaron Whaley directing: “Please prepare a draft response with what we have in the file that is responsive to the request.” The subsequent messages show Sarena and Aaron refining the draft: Sarena compiled a comprehensive list of known providers with any related objections, asked for approval before submission to the District Court (DC), and noted that updated CRRs would be sent to all known providers since many were over a year old.

Across the exchanges, the participants repeatedly referenced the August interrogatories and the need to align the provider list with records in Amy Mills’s file, including whether to include dates of service (DOS) and whether to broaden or narrow the provider list based on the records from 2023 through early 2024. The communications also included standard administrative details such as signatures, office addresses, and contact information for Sarena, with emphasis on her new personal email address. The overall plan culminated in the December 20–23 exchange, with Aaron indicating readiness to file the interrogatories on December 26.

item_84.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-428]. Client: Amy Mills.

This note records correspondence regarding the August interrogatories in the Amy Mills Premise case. The thread begins with an email from Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm to Sarena Tuttle, dated December 20, 2024 at 03:59:13 GMT-05:00. In the message, with Amy Mills’ Filevine project email address CC’d (Amy Mills Premise [DOB-429] - Filevine Project Email Address: [EMAIL-430]), Aaron states: “Ok, then good to go. File them on December 26.”

The preceding exchange began earlier that week and spanned several days of drafting and review. On December 16, 2024 at 12:00 PM, Aaron Whaley asked Sarena to prepare a draft response that would address the August interrogatories using the materials currently in the file. Sarena Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager for The Whaley Law Firm, replied on December 17, 2024 at 11:51 AM. She explained that the draft would list all known providers and any related objections, and that she would obtain approval before submitting to the district agency (DC). She noted that most of the known provider records’ CRRs would need updating, given that many were over a year old. Her signature block identified her as Sarena M. Tuttle and provided her contact information.

On December 17, 2024 at 12:12 PM, Aaron responded that the draft looked good and asked whether the provider list covered all providers since January 1, 2023. Sarena confirmed in another message later that day (12:21 PM) that the draft indeed covered every provider listed in Amy Mills’ file, with most recent CMRs from 2023 through March 2024, while acknowledging that this did not necessarily mean Amy Mills saw those providers in 2023 or 2024. She asked whether Aaron would like her to review the records further and narrow the list accordingly. Aaron agreed that the list seemed to cover all providers since [DOB-431], as reflected in his earlier inquiry.

In a December 19, 2024 email, Sarena delivered a draft for review. She stated she had listed all known providers and any related objections, and requested Aaron’s review for approval before submitting to the DC. She also noted that she would issue updated CRRs to all known providers, recognizing that most CRRs were over a year old. Her message again included her standard signature with her title and contact information.

The thread continued with a December 20, 2024 message from Sarena noting a formatting error she observed: “I saw that. It is a formatting error. When I open on my computer it is not there, I will verify proper formatting on the PDF version upon Approval.” Later that day, Aaron replied with a succinct directive: “Ok, then good to go. File them on December 26.”

Accompanying the chain were the standard email footers and signatures for both counsel, repeatedly showing their professional titles, the Whaley Law Firm address (712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222), multiple phone numbers (D: [PHONE-2551]; O: [PHONE-2550] ext 104; general line [PHONE-2549]), and email contact details (Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com and [EMAIL-435]). The messages also circulated the case’s Filevine-related CC address for Amy Mills Premise, underscoring that the matter centers on the August interrogatories for the Amy Mills Premise case, with a file designation of Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-436] and the client listed as Amy Mills. The correspondence confirms that the plan was to file the related documents on December 26.

item_85.note: Case: Amy Mills — Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-438]
Client: Amy Mills

This note summarizes a December 2024 email thread about the August interrogatories and the provider list for the case of Amy Mills. The primary correspondence ran among Sarena M. Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager at The Whaley Law Firm, and Aaron Whaley, with Amy Mills Premise in copy, related to preparing a draft response and compiling a list of providers.

The thread began with an email from Sarena on December 16, 2024, in which Aaron Whaley urged her to prepare a draft response using the information already in the file that would be responsive to the August interrogatories. The message asked for a draft to address the request directly. This exchange was part of a broader effort to respond to the district court’s (DC) inquiry and to ensure that the file would be properly supported by updated or corrected records.

On December 17, 2024, Sarena sent a draft in response to Aaron’s request. She stated that she had listed all known providers and noted any related objections, adding that she would also obtain updated CRRs for all known providers since most CRRs were over a year old. She stated that the draft was intended for approval before submission to the district court.

Later on December 17, 2024, Aaron replied, asking for clarification: “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-439]?” A few minutes later, at 12:12 PM, Aaron again weighed in, and Sarena responded at 12:21 PM, explaining that the draft included every provider listed in the client’s file, most of whose CMRs dated from 2023 through March 2024. She cautioned that this did not necessarily mean the client saw those providers in 2023 or 2024 and offered to review the records to narrow the list if needed.

At 12:38 PM on December 17, 2024, Aaron replied, “Yes, please do.” In response, Sarena indicated that she would proceed with reviewing the records to narrow the list to those actually seen by the client, while continuing to list all known providers and any objections.

On December 19, 2024, Sarena sent an updated version. She wrote, “Here you go. :) I listed the known providers for 2023 per the records we have. I also included DOS which I do not normally do. Let me know if there are any changes needed.” This message included her standard professional signature with her title, firm, address, telephone numbers, and her note about a new email address.

On December 20, 2024, Aaron commented that in the provider list item “a” it looked blank and suggested it could be a formatting issue. He again referenced the formatting concern in his message.

Shortly after, at 12:05:01 PM on December 20, 2024 (GMT-05:00), Sarena replied that she had observed the same formatting issue. She explained that the blank appeared due to a formatting error and stated that when she opens the PDF on her computer the item is not missing. She said she would verify proper formatting on the PDF version upon approval. Sarena again included her contact information and noted her new email address.

The CC for the primary message included Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2543] with the Filevine project email address [EMAIL-441], indicating that Amy Mills was kept in the loop for these communications. The correspondence illustrates a collaborative effort to finalize the August interrogatories response and ensure an accurate, properly formatted provider list, with ongoing checks for formatting accuracy in the PDF version before final submission to the district court.

item_86.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-444]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: August interrogatories. The note details a chain of email exchanges at The Whaley Law Firm concerning the provider list to be used in response to August interrogatories for Amy Mills.

On December 20, 2024, Aaron Whaley emailed Sarena Tuttle regarding the August interrogatories in the Amy Mills file. In his message he noted that in the provider list item a) the entry appears blank and suggested that this could simply be a formatting issue. The email was sent from Aaron Whaley of the Whaley Law Firm to Sarena Tuttle, with the case’s Filevine project email address cc’d: Amy Mills Premise [DOB-445] - Filevine Project Email Address [EMAIL-446]. The message thread also shows the case context and subject line “Re: August interrogatories.”

In a prior message, dated December 19, 2024, Sarena Tuttle replied to Aaron with the draft provider information. She wrote, “Here you go. :) I listed the known providers for 2023 per the records we have. I also included DOS which I do not normally do. Let me know if there are any changes needed.” Her signature identified her as Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal & Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, and the contact numbers D: [PHONE-2536] and O: [PHONE-2535] ext 104, plus the note that the email address is Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com and an indication to note the new email address.

Earlier in the thread, on December 17, 2024, Aaron Whaley replied, “Yes, please do.” In a contemporaneous message, Sarena asked whether she should review Amy Mills’s records to narrow the provider list, noting that the list includes every provider listed in the file and that most of their most recent CMRs are from 2023 through March 2024, while cautioning that this does not necessarily mean the patient saw those providers in 2023 or 2024. Her message, like the others, was signed with her department title and contact information.

Also on December 17, 2024, at 12:12 PM, Aaron Whaley commented, “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-449]?” The thread also contains an earlier message from December 17, 2024, at 11:51 AM from Sarena, titled “Good afternoon AW,” which stated: “Here is the draft you requested. I listed all known providers and any related objections, please review for approval before submitting to the DC. I will get updated CRRs out to all known providers as well since most are over a year old.” The signatures in these messages again identified Sarena M. Tuttle and provided her contact details at The Whaley Law Firm.

The chain begins with a December 16, 2024, 12:00 PM message from Aaron Whaley requesting the preparation of a draft response with what is in the file that is responsive to the request, followed by the subsequent December 17–20 exchanges in which the provider list and related considerations were discussed and drafted for submission to the DC. Throughout, the correspondence lists the firm’s standard contact details for Sarena Tuttle, including the firm address, phone numbers, and her email, and includes the case’s Filevine project email address in the cc field for tracking and project management purposes.

item_87.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-451]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: August interrogatories.

On December 19, 2024 at 12:23:58 PM (GMT-05:00), Sarena Tuttle, a litigation paralegal and workers’ compensation case manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email regarding the August interrogatories to Aaron Whaley, with Amy Mills Premise [DOB-452] included in the CC line via the Filevine project email address. The subject was “Re: August interrogatories.” In the body of the message, Sarena wrote: “Here you go. I listed the known providers for 2023 per the records we have. I also included DOS which I do not normally do. Let me know if there are any changes needed.” Sarena signed with her full title and contact information, noting the new email address: Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal & Workers' Compensation Case Manager, The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. D: [PHONE-2530]; O: [PHONE-2529] ext 104; E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com.

Earlier in the thread, several messages circulated between Sarena and Aaron around December 16–17, 2024:

- On December 16, 2024 at 12:00 PM, Aaron Whaley wrote: “Please prepare a draft response with what we have in the file that is responsive to the request.”

- On December 17, 2024 at 11:51 AM, Sarena replied: “Good afternoon AW, Here is the draft you requested. I listed all known providers and any related objections, please review for approval before submitting to the DC. I will get updated CRRs out to all known providers as well since most are over a year old.” Her signature followed with the same contact details and note about the new email address.

- On December 17, 2024 at 12:12 PM, Aaron replied: “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-455]?”

- On December 17, 2024 at 12:21 PM, Sarena responded: “It is every provider listed in her file, most of which our most recent CMRs are from 2023 through March 2024. However, that is not to say she saw the providers in 2023/2024. Would you like me to review her records & narrow it down?” She again signed with her title and contact details and the note about the new email address.

- On December 17, 2024 at 12:38 PM, Aaron replied: “Yes, please do.”

- The thread then includes Sarena’s December 17, 2024 message reiterating that she would compile all known providers and any related objections for the draft, to be submitted to the DC, and that updated CRRs would be sent to all known providers since most are over a year old, followed by her signature with the same contact information and new email note.

The overall thread shows the team discussing and preparing a draft response to interrogatories, with the main decision points being whether to list all providers, whether to narrow the list to those actually seen in 2023/2024, and the plan to issue current CRRs to providers. The December 19, 2024 email from Sarena confirms the draft list of known providers for 2023 (including DOS) and asks Aaron to indicate any needed changes. The communication repeatedly references that most of the recent CMRs are from 2023 through March 2024, and it notes that listing all providers does not necessarily mean Amy Mills saw all those providers in 2023 or 2024. The tone throughout is one of collaboration and review prior to submitting to the DC, with an emphasis on updating CRRs for providers where they are over a year old.

item_88.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-457]. Client: Amy Mills. This matter is Mills v. Forcht Bank, 20-CI-112, Knox Circuit Court, Division II, and the file concerns Amy Mills as the client.

On December 16, 2024, Amanda Murphy of the Kentucky courts system sent an email regarding Mills v. Forcht Bank to Betsy Catron, with multiple recipients copied. The message, dated Monday, December 16, 2024, at 16:50:22 UTC, was from Amanda Murphy ([EMAIL-458]) to Betsy Catron ([EMAIL-459]), with the following individuals copied: Elizabeth Romersa ([EMAIL-460]), Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-2522]), Sarena Tuttle (sarena@whaleylawfirm.com), Sarena Tuttle ([EMAIL-2521]), and [EMAIL-463]. The body of that email simply contained the word “Thank you!” The message appeared in an email chain that began with Betsy Catron responding to Amanda Murphy, and the note included “Get Outlook for iOS.”

Earlier that same day, at 11:49:08 AM, Betsy R. Catron, Attorney at WHT Law Center, Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, sent an email to Amanda Murphy with the subject Mills v. Forcht Bank, 20-CI-112, Knox Circuit Court, Division II. In her message, Betsy wrote: “Hi Amanda, Please see attached order on the Defendant’s Motion to Compel in 20-CI-112, Amy Mills v Forcht Bank, reflecting the court’s ruling at the hearing this morning and prepared pursuant to the Court’s direction. This will be e-filed as well according to the instructions provided to me today on the purple sheet. Please do not hesitate to contact me with any questions regarding the attached. Thank you, Betsy R. Catron.” Her signature then followed, identifying her as an attorney at WHT Law Center, with the Vine Center address and contact information: 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507; P [PHONE-464]; F [PHONE-465]; www.whtlaw.com. The email also included a standard note stating that the message originated from outside the Kentucky Courts and advising not to click links or open attachments unless the sender and content are recognized as safe.

The Betsy email indicated that an attached order was being provided concerning the Defendant’s Motion to Compel, in the 20-CI-112 matter, reflecting the court’s ruling at the hearing that occurred that morning. The order was prepared pursuant to the Court’s direction, and Betsy stated it would be filed electronically as well in accordance with instructions she received on the purple sheet. She invited Amanda to contact her with any questions regarding the attachment.

item_89.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-2516]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: August interrogatories. This note captures a December 2024 email exchange between Aaron Whaley and Sarena M. Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm, with Amy Mills Premise [DOB-2515] listed in the Filevine project email CC as [EMAIL-469], concerning a draft response to the August interrogatories.

The thread begins with Aaron Whaley replying to a prior thread on December 17, 2024 at 12:38:11 PM GMT-05:00, writing simply, “Yes, please do.” Earlier that day, at 12:21 PM (time shown as 12:21?PM in the thread), Sarena M. Tuttle sent a message explaining that the list of providers under consideration includes every provider listed in Amy Mills’ file, and that most of the latest CMRs are from 2023 through March 2024. She cautioned, however, that this does not necessarily mean Amy Mills saw those providers in 2023 or 2024, and she asked whether Aaron would like her to review the records and narrow the list down.

Prior to that, at 12:12 PM on December 17, 2024 (time shown as 12:12?PM), Aaron asked, “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-470]?” In an earlier message from 11:51 AM on December 17, 2024 (time shown as 11:51?AM), Sarena sent the draft Aaron had requested. She stated that she had listed all known providers and any related objections, and asked for approval before submitting to the DC. She also noted that she would send updated CRRs to all known providers since most are over a year old.

The chain traces back to an initial instruction on December 16, 2024 at 12:00 PM (time shown as 12:00?PM), when Aaron instructed Sarena to prepare a draft response with what they have in the file that is responsive to the request.

The thread closes with the typical firm signatures from Aaron Whaley and Sarena M. Tuttle, including their contact details. The messages reference The Whaley Law Firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with phone numbers listed as D: [PHONE-2512] and O: [PHONE-2511] ext 104 for Sarena, and [PHONE-473] for Aaron. Sarena notes her role as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager and includes her email, Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, while also indicating a new email address. The overall context is a coordinated effort to prepare a responsive draft regarding the August interrogatories, including consideration of which providers to include and the timing of updated CRRs.

item_90.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-475]. Client: Amy Mills.

Re: August interrogatories. On December 17, 2024, at 12:21:40 PM (GMT-05:00), an email was sent from Sarena Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm to Aaron Whaley, with a copy to the Amy Mills Premise [DOB-476] Filevine project email address. The subject line indicated the matter concerned the August interrogatories. In the body of the message, Sarena explained that the list included every provider listed in Amy Mills’s file, noting that most of the firm’s most recent CMRs (case medical reports) were from 2023 through March 2024. She added that this did not necessarily mean Amy had seen those providers in 2023 or 2024, and asked whether Aaron would like her to review Amy’s records and narrow the list.

Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, provided her contact details and signature, including the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, with direct line [PHONE-2506] and office line [PHONE-2505] ext 104. She noted a new email address: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com.

Earlier in the thread, Aaron Whaley had replied on December 17, 2024, at 12:12? PM, asking: “That looks good. The list of providers is all provider since [DOB-479]?”

Prior to that, on December 17, 2024, at 11:51? AM, Sarena sent a draft message to AW. She wrote that she had prepared the draft you requested, listing all known providers and any related objections, and asked AW to review for approval before submitting to the DC. She also stated that she would send updated CRRs to all known providers since most were over a year old.

The chain also shows an email from Aaron Whaley on December 16, 2024 at 12:00? PM, requesting: “Please prepare a draft response with what we have in the file that is responsive to the request.” The thread then closes with Aaron’s signature block repeating his contact information at The Whaley Law Firm.

In sum, the correspondence from mid-December 2024 centers on preparing a draft response to the August interrogatories, with a comprehensive list of providers drawn from Amy Mills’s file (noting that most current medical reports were dated 2023 through March 2024), a plan to review and potentially narrow the list, and a plan to issue updated CRRs to providers given that many are over a year old. The exchange includes confirmations of the draft provider list, discussion of any objections, and the intent to move forward with submission to the district court, along with acknowledgement of the new email address for Sarena.

Wait — the signature shows WHT Law, Vine Center, Lexington, Kentucky, sent an email from Betsy Catron regarding Amy Mills in the case Mills v. Forcht Bank, docket 20-CI-112 in Knox Circuit Court, Division II. The email concerned Amy Mills as the client and was addressed to Amanda Murphy at [EMAIL-2467], with the following people copied: Elizabeth Romersa ([EMAIL-517]), Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-518]), Sarena Tuttle (sarena@whaleylawfirm.com), Sarena Tuttle ([EMAIL-519]), and [EMAIL-520].

The body of the message begins with “Hi Amanda.” Catron asks Amanda to please see the attached order on the Defendant's Motion to Compel in 20-CI-112, Mills v. Forcht Bank, noting that the attached order reflects the court’s ruling at the hearing held that morning and was prepared pursuant to the court’s direction. She adds that the order will also be e-filed in accordance with the instructions provided to her that day on the purple sheet. Catron invites Amanda to contact her with any questions regarding the attached document and closes with thanks.

The signature block identifies Betsy R. Catron as Attorney, followed by the firm’s branding and contact information: Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507; phone [PHONE-2462]; fax [PHONE-2461]; website www.whtlaw.com. The message also includes a logo line labeled [WHT_EmailLogo]. The message was sent at 16:49:08 UTC on December 16, 2024.
