item_320.note: Case: Amy Mills premises note for Amy Mills, dated [DOB-1515], documents a sequence of email communications on October 6 and October 16, 2023 between Amy Mills’ attorney and Bluegrass Orthopaedics regarding a telephone conference with Dr. Wallace Huff.

The involved parties were Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm ([EMAIL-1516]), and Anna Mercer, the clinical assistant to Dr. Wallace Huff at Bluegrass Orthopaedics ([EMAIL-1517]). Amy Mills was CC’d on the correspondence. The note includes standard confidentiality notices typical of these emails.

The first exchange took place on Friday, October 6, 2023. Anna Mercer indicated that Bluegrass Orthopaedics could arrange a phone conference with Amy Mills’ attorney and Dr. Huff, noting the cost: $1,000 per 30 minutes, with these telephone conferences typically held on Tuesday afternoons. Aaron Whaley replied that he was free on Tuesday and that would work for him.

Later that same day, Anna replied that they could accommodate a phone call with Aaron and Dr. Huff, confirming the rate of $1,000 per 30 minutes and the typical Tuesday afternoon scheduling.

A follow-up thread on October 16, 2023 begins with Aaron Whaley’s confirmation that 10/24/23 would work for him. Anna then announced that she had scheduled the telephone conference for [DOB-1518] at 3:45 p.m. and asked for a fax number to which the invoice could be sent; she also stated that payment would need to be received 48 business hours before the conference to avoid cancellation or postponement.

That same day, Aaron Whaley responded that 10/24/23 works for him. Earlier that morning, Anna had suggested Dr. Huff could do either [DOB-1519] at 3:45 p.m. or [DOB-1520] at 3:30 p.m., and asked Aaron to indicate which date would be preferred so she could send the invoice.

The note also lists the Bluegrass Orthopaedics contact details for reference: 3401 Yorkshire Medical Park, Lexington, KY 40509; phone [PHONE-1521], extension 226; and the website www.bluegrassortho.com, along with social media links (Facebook, Twitter). The Whaley Law Firm signatures include their office address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222 and phone number [PHONE-1522].

In addition to these procedural details, the emails include the standard confidentiality notices required by Electronic Communications Privacy Act (18 U.S.C. 2510-2521), cautioning that the information is confidential and intended only for the named recipients, with instructions to notify the sender and delete the message if received in error.

item_321.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premises file ([DOB-1524]). The matter concerns coordinating a telephone conference with Dr. Wallace Huff for Amy Mills. The communications involved Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm and Anna Mercer, a clinical assistant to Dr. Huff at Bluegrass Orthopaedics, with Amy Mills cc’d at [EMAIL-1525].

The exchange began on October 6, 2023. Aaron Whaley wrote that he was free that Tuesday if a phone discussion would work. Anna Mercer replied that a telephone conference with Dr. Huff could be arranged, noting that the conference costs $1,000 per 30 minutes and that they typically conduct such calls on Tuesday afternoons.

On October 16, 2023, Anna informed Whaley that Dr. Huff would be available for a conference on October 24, 2023 at 3:45 p.m. or on October 31, 2023 at 3:30 p.m., and she asked which date would work best so she could send the invoice. On October 24, 2023, Aaron Whaley replied that 10/24/23 works for him, ending the exchange with “10/24/23 Works for me. Thank you.”

The result of the thread was a confirmed plan to conduct the telephone conference with Dr. Huff on October 24, 2023 at 3:45 p.m. The agreed rate remained $1,000 per 30 minutes, in line with their standard practice of scheduling these calls on Tuesday afternoons.

For context and contact details: Anna Mercer serves as the clinical assistant to Dr. Wallace Huff at Bluegrass Orthopaedics, located at 3401 Yorkshire Medical Park, Lexington, KY 40509. The office can be reached at [PHONE-1526], Ext. 226, and more information is available on their website. Aaron Whaley is with Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, and can be reached at [PHONE-1527].

Both sides included standard confidentiality notices with their emails, identifying the message as covered by the Electronic Communications Privacy Act (18 U.S.C. 2510-2521) and indicating that the information is confidential and intended only for the named recipient. If received in error, recipients were instructed to notify the sender by telephone and delete the message.

item_329.note: This note concerns the case Amy Mills (Amy Mills Premise, [DOB-1546]) and the client Amy Mills, with a topic of Re: Annuity info. The exchange records email correspondence from Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm, including communication details and recipient addresses.

On October 12, 2023 at 14:57:19 -0400, Aaron Whaley, [EMAIL-1547], sent an email with the subject Re: Annuity info. The message was addressed to [EMAIL-1548], Amy Mills, and [EMAIL-1549]; Amy Mills is listed twice in the To field. In the body of the email, he wrote: “I apologize; somehow I missed it. Here it is. Let me know if you have any questions.”

Prior to that, on October 11, 2023 at about 8:36 PM, Amy Mills replied: “I did get the email but is there a #2 that was sent? It goes from 1 to 3 and no 2?”

Earlier still, on September 21, 2023 at 12:15 PM, Aaron Whaley had written: “Here is the information you requested. Let me know if you need anything else or want to talk.”

The record includes the sender’s and firm’s contact details. The closing signature for Aaron Whaley appears after the message as:

-- 
Aaron Whaley
Whaley Law Firm
712 Lyndon Ln
Louisville, KY 40222
[PHONE-1550]

This signature block is present twice in the record.

item_331.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1553]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: A. Mills. On October 6, 2023, an email thread regarding Amy Mills circulated between Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm and Anna Mercer of Bluegrass Orthopaedics, with Amy Mills copied. The message from Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-1554]) was sent at 12:44:57 PM Eastern Daylight Time on Friday, October 6, 2023, to Anna Mercer ([EMAIL-1555]) and Amy Mills ([EMAIL-1556]). In his note, Aaron wrote: “Sounds great. I am free this Tuesday if that works.”

Earlier that same day, at 9:36 AM (indicated as 9:36?AM) on Friday, October 6, 2023, Anna Mercer replied: “Good morning! We would definitely be able to do a phone call with you and Dr. Huff. A telephone conference is $1000 per 30 minutes. We do these on Tuesday afternoons typically.” Her signature identified her as the Clinical Assistant to Dr. Wallace Huff at Bluegrass Orthopaedics, located at 3401 Yorkshire Medical Park, Lexington, KY 40509. The signature included the office phone number [PHONE-1557] Ext. 226, an office fax line placeholder, and the website www.bluegrassortho.com, along with references to Facebook and Twitter and a Website. The message also contained a Confidentiality Notice stating: “NOTICE OF CONFIDENTIALITY: This e-mail is covered by the Electronic Communications Privacy Act, 18 U.S.C. Section 2510-2521 and is legally privileged. The information contained herein is confidential information intended only for the use of the individual or entity named above. If the reader of this message is not the intended recipient, or if an attachment is made in error, the reader is hereby notified that any dissemination, distribution or copying of this communication is strictly prohibited. If you have received this transmission in error, please notify the above person by telephone immediately by return e-mail and delete/trash the original message from your system. Thank you for your cooperation.”

The correspondence was part of a note in the Amy Mills file, with the contributing contacts listed as Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm (712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222; [PHONE-1558]) and the Bluegrass Orthopaedics team represented by Anna Mercer.

item_332.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1560]. Client: Amy Mills. This is in the matter of Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank.

On October 5, 2023, communications regarding Amy Mills’ medical records took place between Betsy R. Catron, attorney at Vine Center in Lexington, KY, and Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm in Louisville, KY, in connection with the Amy Mills case. Betsy requested that Aaron submit the additional medical records and bills related to Amy Mills’ recent medical treatment, and she included her firm’s contact information in the message. Amy Mills and Sarena Tuttle were copied on the correspondence.

Earlier that morning, Betsy had written, “Hi Aaron, [Can you please submit the additional records and bills for your client for her recent medical treatment?] Thank you,” followed by her signature block which listed Vine Center, 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, with telephone and fax numbers and the website www.whtlaw.com.

In Aaron’s subsequent reply, sent at approximately 11:02 AM, he stated, “Betsy, happy to. What is the last date you have records for?” This indicates the request for the timeframe of the records.

Later that day, at 11:08:59 AM, Aaron sent another message to Betsy with the subject content starting, “Betsy, I’ll make it easy. Here is a link to a dropbox folder with every medical record and bill that I have. Sorted by date.” He provided a Dropbox link: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/1893hm62npxoyf8/AAA-a48rECj5IP5XyKkKv2WJa?dl=0. In this latest message, Betsy was copied along with Amy Mills and Sarena Tuttle.

The signature blocks accompanying the messages included contact details for Betsy Catron at Vine Center and for Aaron Whaley at Whaley Law Firm. Betsy’s signature listed Vine Center’s address and contact information, while Aaron’s signature provided his own firm address and phone number.

item_343.note: On September 21, 2023 at 12:12:52 PM (ET), Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm sent an email regarding Amy Mills. The message was from [EMAIL-1585] and was addressed to sarena@whaleylawfirm.com (Sarena Tuttle) and [EMAIL-1586] (Bryce Koon), with [EMAIL-1587] (Amy Mills) CC’d. In the body, Whaley wrote that he could not get it done that day and asked the recipients to look at Amy Mills’ project in Filevine, noting that he had created a timeline. He stated that if there are no records attached, then they don’t have them and will need them, and he pointed out that there are a lot of bills they don’t have. He indicated that he had obtained many of the bills from the Equian lien pulled from Zipliens. He requested updated records from her PCP and from PT Pros, and said they needed to reach out to her doctor at Bluegrass Orthopaedics, Dr. Wallace Huff, to see if he would provide a Narrative and to determine what surgery he is suggesting and what the cost would be.

The message ends with the following signature: Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, phone [PHONE-1588].

item_345.note: Case: Amy Mills Premise-[DOB-1593]. This note concerns Mills v. Forcht Bank and relates to mediation in the Mills Premise case, with the client being Amy Mills.

On September 20, 2023, at 1:03 PM (Eastern Time), an email from Aaron Whaley of the Whaley Law Firm was sent regarding Mills v. Forcht Bank Mediation. The message was addressed to [EMAIL-1594] and to [EMAIL-1595] (Amy Mills). The date on the email was Wednesday, September 20, 2023, 13:03:26 -0400. In the body of the message, Whaley wrote: “Judge, please see the attached demand that has previously been sent to defense counsel. This should give you a pretty good idea of the case and our damages. There has been no response to our demand other than to schedule mediation so when we start tomorrow the ball is in their court.” The email was signed with the closing “All the best,” followed by Aaron Whaley’s name and affiliation with the Whaley Law Firm, including the firm’s contact details: 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, and [PHONE-1596].

item_346.note: This note concerns the case labeled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1599], with the client Amy Mills. On September 18, 2023, at 10:03:39 AM Eastern Time, Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email titled “Your Mediation Zoom Link - September 21, 2023 9:00a ET” from sarena@whaleylawfirm.com to Amy Mills at [EMAIL-1600], with cc copies to Aaron Whaley at [EMAIL-1601] and Bryce Koon at [EMAIL-1602].

In the body of the message, Saraena stated good morning to Amy and provided the Zoom link for the mediation. She confirmed that the mediation would take place on September 21, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time. The email explained that this session would give Amy the opportunity to discuss her claim with a former circuit court judge and the Defense Counsel. The mediator would review a summary of the evidence, listen to both sides and their current positions, and then offer insight into how the claim should be resolved. The mediator’s opinions are not final and are intended only as a tool to help decide whether to move forward with trial or to pursue settlement. The email also noted that if the Defense is willing, a settlement might be reached if there is mutual agreement; if not, the claim would proceed to trial. Amy was invited to contact the sender if she had any questions or needed further discussion.

The message also provided the exact meeting time as Sep 21, 2023, 09:00 AM Eastern Time (US and Canada) and included the Zoom joining details: Join Zoom Meeting https://us02web.zoom.us/j/[PHONE-1603], Meeting ID [PHONE-1604], Passcode 54321.

The signature block identified Sarena M. Tuttle as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. It listed D: [PHONE-1605], O: [PHONE-1606] ext 104, E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, and included the note: (PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS).

item_352.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1642]. Client: Amy Mills.

On September 18, 2023 at 12:44:09 p.m. Eastern Time, Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm forwarded an email titled “Fwd: Download Facebook” to multiple recipients, including [EMAIL-1643], Amy Mills, [EMAIL-1644], [EMAIL-1645], and Amy Mills again. In the forwarded message, Aaron stated that, in preparation for mediation that week, he wanted to review Amy Mills’s Facebook history, specifically the period before her fall, in order to contrast the before-and-after. He asked to receive Amy’s best pre-fall and post-fall pictures, emphasizing normal, social pictures, and directed that the information be downloaded as part of the process.

The forwarded content included a prior message from Sarena M. Tuttle dated September 14, 2023, at 9:44 a.m., with the subject “Re: Download Facebook,” sent to Bryce Koon and cc’d to Aaron Whaley. Sarena noted that if Amy were using a desktop—which she recommended—it would be a little different and provided the following steps: click the upper right profile picture, click Settings & Privacy, click Your Facebook Information, and click Download. The message was accompanied by three image attachments. Sarena signed as follows:

Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal & Workers' Compensation Case Manager, The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. D: [PHONE-1646], O: [PHONE-1647] ext 104, E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com (Please note new email address).

Earlier in the thread, on September 14, 2023 at 9:30 a.m., Bryce Koon responded that he hadn’t done it before but recalled seeing the instructions recently in someone’s discovery requests. Prior to that exchange, Aaron Whaley had sent a message on September 14, 2023 at 9:26 a.m. to Bryce Koon and Sarena Tuttle with the subject “Download Facebook,” asking whether either of them knew how to download Amy Mills’s Facebook history, and stating a desire to retrieve her history going back as far as possible to determine a contrast with the period before the fall.

The thread closes with Aaron Whaley’s signature: Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222, [PHONE-1648].

item_356.note: In the case Amy Mills, docketed as Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1657], the client is Amy Mills. The mediation has been scheduled for September 21, 2023 at 10:00 a.m. The notice appears to come from The Whaley Law Firm, and the schedule was communicated to the client via email.

On August 24, 2023, at 10:12:52 a.m. (Eastern Time), Sarena Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, sent an email from sarena@whaleylawfirm.com to Amy Mills at [EMAIL-1658], with Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-1659]) and Bryce Koon ([EMAIL-1660]) CC’ed. The email stated: "Good morning Amy, Please find attached our office's notice of mediation scheduling. As we discussed, your mediation will take place on September 21, 2023 at 10:00am via Zoom Conference. Hon. Judge Thomas Knopf will provide us with a Zoom link once we get a little closer to the mediation date. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us directly to discuss them in further detail." The message was signed by Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager, The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Direct dial: [PHONE-1661]; Office: [PHONE-1662] ext 104; Email: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note: PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.

item_363.note: This note concerns the case identified as Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1683], involving client Amy Mills, in the Knox Circuit Court as Mills v. Forcht Bank. The case is filed under NCP KNOX 20-CI-00112, with envelope number 6477128.

The circuit clerk has processed and accepted the filing. The filing was processed on August 23, 2023, at 8:00 AM Eastern Time. The eFiler listed is Betsy R. Catron, attorney for the defendant. The court handling the matter is Knox Circuit Court. The case caption is Mills, Amy vs. Forcht Bank, and the case number remains 20-CI-00112. The envelope number is 6477128.

Notice of the filing was electronically mailed to the following recipients: Aaron Gregory Whaley at [EMAIL-1684], Betsy R. Catron at [EMAIL-1685], and Joshua Ellis at [EMAIL-1686]. There were no scheduled events noted for this filing.

The documents included in this eFiling are listed as NOTICE - OTHER, and the document can be viewed at the Kentucky Court of Justice eFiling retrieval link: https://kcoj.kycourts.net/eFilingRetrieval/Home/Package?id=02A55EFA-DB8A-4175-BBE3-50AECC5B508C. This notification was automatically generated by the Kentucky Court of Justice eFiling system, and correspondence should not be sent in reply.

Kentucky Court of Justice Confidentiality Notice accompanies this message, stating that the content is intended only for the addressee and may contain privileged, confidential, and/or proprietary work product. If you are not the intended recipient, or an authorized employee, agent, or representative of the intended recipient, you should not read, copy, retain, or disseminate this message or any attachment, and you should not forward it without the sender’s express written consent. If you have received this message in error, you are instructed to contact the sender immediately and delete all copies of the message and any attachments. Transmission or misdelivery does not waive any applicable legal privilege.

item_366.note: On Monday, August 21, 2023, at approximately 12:46 PM, Betsy R. Catron, an attorney at Vine Center (WHT Law), wrote to Aaron Whaley regarding mediation dates for Amy Mills v. Forcht Bank. She noted that she and the judge’s mediation calendar, as well as her own schedule, indicated several potential all-day mediation dates: September 21 (all day), October 23 (all day), October 26 (all day), and October 27 (all day). She asked Aaron to advise regarding his availability, adding a preference for the morning if possible. The message was signed by Betsy R. Catron, Attorney, with contact information for Vine Center: 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507; P [PHONE-1698]; F [PHONE-1699]; www.whtlaw.com.

The following day, Tuesday, August 22, 2023, at 2:46:29 PM -0400, Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, replied to Mrs. Catron. She stated that their office and Mrs. Mills are available for mediation on September 21, 2023, starting at 10:00 AM, and that they prefer a Zoom conference due to the distance of all parties involved. She asked Mrs. Catron to confirm whether this schedule works for her and her client. The message included her signature with full contact details: Sarena M. Tuttle, The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; D: [PHONE-1700]; O: [PHONE-1701] ext 104; E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com (PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS). The email was CC’d to [EMAIL-1702], Aaron Whaley, and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1703]. The subject line of the thread was Re: Amy Mills v Forcht Bank mediation.

item_368.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1707]. Client: Amy Mills.

On Monday, August 21, 2023, at 3:17:09 PM Eastern Daylight Time, Baptist Health EFT Server sent an automated invitation to sarena@whaleylawfirm.com, inviting them to a shared folder named “Whaley Law Firm 185511834.” If this was the recipient’s first time accessing the portal at https://baptistftp.bhsi.com, they would be required to set up a password, and they were instructed to use the email address to which this message was sent as their username. The invitation to view the shared folder would expire if not verified within 30 days of receipt, and the recipient was directed to use the “View Folder” link to verify access and view the shared files. The sender included a note: “A MILLS-185511834-EXPIRES WITHIN 5 DAYS.”

This is an automated message. Any support issues or questions should be directed by replying to this email, though responses may take up to 5 business days. For questions regarding the information shared, it is recommended to contact the Baptist Health department that originally sent the invitation. A Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) link for accessing files from Baptist Health EFT was provided.

This transmission may contain information that is privileged, confidential, and/or exempt from disclosure under applicable law. If you are not the intended recipient, please delete this message, and you are hereby notified that any disclosure, copying, distribution, or use of the information contained herein (including any reliance thereon) is strictly prohibited. Baptist Health will never send emails that require you to send account or personal information via public email or pop-up windows.

This email, facsimile, or letter and any files or attachments transmitted with it contains information that is confidential and proprietary. This information is intended only for the use of the individual(s) and entity(ies) to whom it is addressed. If you are the intended recipient, further disclosures are prohibited without proper authorization. If you are not the intended recipient, any disclosure, copying, printing, or use of this information is strictly prohibited and possibly a violation of federal or state law. If you have received this information in error, please notify Baptist Health immediately at [PHONE-1708] or via email to the sender.
item_368.note_summary: Mills | . Client: Amy Mills. | 2023-08-22

item_369.note: This case is labeled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1710] and concerns the client Amy Mills.

On Friday, August 18, 2023, at 19:55:29 UTC, Stephanie Mason, ROI Quality Analyst ([EMAIL-1711]), sent an email from [EMAIL-1712] to SARENA@whaleylawfirm.com regarding a file for Amy Mills. The message includes a file named AMY MILLS.pdf with a direct access link: https://medicopy.box.com/s/xcsshw7v8drclrrxp0bnhpgrfujmera9, and a View link: https://medicopy.box.com/s/xcsshw7v8drclrrxp0bnhpgrfujmera9.

The body of the email states: “Please click ‘View File’ to access the medical records you have requested. The password for access will be ‘MCSI’ plus the patient’s four-digit birth year (i.e., MCSI1912). Please download the file, as the link will disable in 10 days. If you need further assistance, please contact our office at [PHONE-1713]. Thank you.” It also includes a prompt to view the file and a note to download, since the link will expire in 10 days, along with the instruction to contact the office for assistance.

The email provides a mobile viewing option with a box app link: https://app.box.com/link/?lp=... (an extended mobile link is included). It also repeats the Box header/footer context, including the standard Box© footer with the address “© 2023 | 900 Jefferson Avenue, Redwood City, CA 94063, USA” and links labeled About Box, Privacy Policy, and related settings (e.g., Edit Notification Settings). The footer lists “MediCopy” and ends with: “To unsubscribe from these emails please click the following link:,” though no unsubscribe link is shown in the note provided.

Forcht Bank, with Case Number 20-CI-00112 and Envelope Number 6422678. item_386.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1768]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: Amy Mills Medical Records. The exchange begins with an email thread labeled “Re: Amy Mills Medical Records” in which the matter is being organized and updated for Amy Mills. The original sender in the visible thread is Sarena M. Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm, and the message is dated August 9, 2023.

In the thread, Sarena’s signature identifies her as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with her contact information and a note that there is a new email address. The header shows the message came from Sarena to Aaron Whaley and was cc’d to Bryce Koon, to a file labeled Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1769], and to Jessica Bottorff, with the subject line “Re: Amy Mills Medical Records.”

The body of the thread begins with Sarena replying “Absolutely.” The thread then outlines a sequence of messages exchanged on August 9, 2023. Earlier in the day, at about 11:42 AM, Aaron Whaley asked whether the team could update and clean up the records, ensuring certified records for every provider as well as the associated bills, with all information easily accessible in Amy Mills’s file. He noted that Amy Mills was still receiving treatment from Bluegrass Orthopaedics, specifically Dr. Huff, even though the team had marked treatment as complete, and he requested updated records from Bluegrass because Mills had seen Dr. Huff about a month earlier and had been referred to an orthopedic clinic in Nashville for her back.

Around 11:53 AM, Sarena replied, “I’m on it,” and she signed her message with her full title and contact details. At about 12:09 PM, Sarena wrote that she had reviewed Mills Cert MRs and found they were in the Litigation Folder, properly labeled by Date Recvd and Provider, and that she would send all new requests that day to ensure everything was up to date. She again signed with her title and contact information, including the notice to please note her new email address.

Later, at roughly 1:51 PM, Aaron responded again, thanking Sarena and asking whether she or someone else could also save the medical records to the medical tab, including the bills and the billed amount for each provider. The thread shows the ongoing efforts to organize Amy Mills’s medical records, certify the providers, and ensure that billing information is complete and easily accessible in her file.

Throughout the thread, the communications were directed within The Whaley Law Firm and included the relevant participants in Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1770]’s matter, with Sarena M. Tuttle consistently handling the organization and verification of Mills’s medical records, and Aaron Whaley directing the need for up-to-date, comprehensive documentation. The record also notes that the correspondence comes from and goes to The Whaley Law Firm’s Louisville, Kentucky offices, with Sarena’s signature listing her phone numbers and her new email address.

item_387.note: Case: Amy Mills Premise-[DOB-1772]. Client: Amy Mills.

In the matter of Re: Amy Mills Medical Records, on Wednesday, August 9, 2023, at about 1:51 PM Eastern Time, Aaron Whaley of The Whaley Law Firm sent an email to Sarena Tuttle regarding Amy Mills’ medical records. The message was cc’d to Bryce Koon, the Amy Mills Premise file (Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1773]), and Jessica Bottorff, among others. Aaron asked if someone could also save the medical records to the medical tab with the bills and the billed amount for each provider.

Earlier in the same thread, at 12:09 PM, Sarena Tuttle replied that she had reviewed the Mills Certified Medical Records (MRs) and found them in the Litigation Folder, properly labeled by Date Received and Provider. She stated she would send all new requests that day to ensure everything was up to date. This followed a prior note in the thread where Sarena stated, “I’m on it,” at 11:53 AM.

The preceding message from Aaron, sent at 11:42 AM, requested that the records be updated and cleaned up. He emphasized the need for certified records for every provider as well as the accompanying bills, and he wanted all information easily accessible in Amy Mills’ file. He pointed out that Mills is still treating with Bluegrass Orthopaedics, Dr. Huff, even though he had previously indicated that treatment was complete. Aaron noted that she had seen Dr. Huff about a month ago and had been referred to an orthopedic clinic in Nashville for her back, and he requested updated records from Bluegrass Orthopaedics as a result. The thread ends with Aaron’s signature listing The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, and phone number [PHONE-1774].

Sarena M. Tuttle
Litigation Paralegal & Workers’ Compensation Case Manager
The Whaley Law Firm
712 Lyndon Lane
Louisville, KY 40222
D: [PHONE-1775]
O: [PHONE-1776] ext 104
E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com
(Note: This is marked as the new email address.)

The subject was “Re: Amy Mills Medical Records.”

In the body of the message, Sarena reported that she had reviewed Mills’ certified medical records (Mills Cert MRs) and found that they are located in the Litigation Folder, properly labeled by date received and provider. She stated that she would send all new requests that day to ensure everything is up to date. The message was signed by Sarena M. Tuttle, reiterating her roles as Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her direct line is [PHONE-1781], and the office line is [PHONE-1782] ext. 104. Her email is Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note indicating a new email address.

Earlier in the same thread, around 11:53 AM (time shown as 11:53?AM in the record), Sarena replied, “I’m on it,” continuing her earlier message and signing with the same title, contact information, and reminder to note the new email address.

Prior to that, at about 11:42 AM, Aaron Whaley had written asking for an update and cleanup: he wanted certified records for every provider as well as bills, and for all information to be easily accessible in Amy Mills’ file. He noted that Mills is still treating with Bluegrass Orthopaedics, Dr. Huff, despite a treatment-complete designation, and requested updated records from them since she had seen Dr. Huff about a month earlier and had been referred to an orthopedics clinic in Nashville for her back. The message was signed by Aaron Whaley of The Whaley Law Firm, with the firm’s address and contact information included.

On Wednesday, August 9, 2023, at 11:53:36 AM Eastern Time, an email was sent from Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, to Aaron Whaley with copy to Bryce Koon, Jessica Bottorff, and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1786]. The message concerned Amy Mills’ medical records and carried the subject line “Re: Amy Mills Medical Records.” The header shows the From address as Sarena Tuttle, the To address as [EMAIL-1787], and the CC line including [EMAIL-1788], [EMAIL-1789], and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1790] ([EMAIL-1791]). Sarena’s message simply says, “I’m on it.”

Sarena M. Tuttle’s signature identifies her as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her direct line is [PHONE-1792], and her office line is [PHONE-1793] ext 104. Her email is Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note indicating this is her new email address (PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS).

Earlier that same day, on Wednesday, August 9, 2023, at 11:42? AM (the timestamp shows “11:42?AM”), Aaron Whaley replied with a request to update and clean up the records. His message states: “Can we get this updated and cleaned up? I want to make sure we have certified records for every provider as well as bills. I want all the information easily accessible in her file. Also, she is still treating with Bluegrass Orthopaedics, Dr. Huff, even though I put treatment complete. Need updated records from them as she saw him about a month ago and was referred to an ortho clinic in Nashville for her back.” The email is attributed to Aaron Whaley of The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, with the phone number [PHONE-1794]. The closing signature reads: “-- Aaron Whaley Whaley Law Firm 712 Lyndon Ln Louisville, KY 40222 [PHONE-1795].”

item_391.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise file identified as Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1803]. Client: Amy Mills. The subject of the exchange is a forwarded message titled “FW: Amy Mills.” On August 9, 2023, Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm spoke with Amy Mills by phone to gather additional information. Amy stated she does not have any documents related to the jobs at issue, with the most recent position occurring eight years ago. Aaron added the information Amy could remember, and in the accompanying note Bryce Koon instructed to finish up and add something noting that Amy cannot recall specific details like phone numbers, with a plan to supplement if those memories change. The note also states that, aside from what Amy recalled, this is good for filing. Amy has not applied for any jobs in the relevant area since her last job, and there is nothing responsive to the first Request for Production (RPD).

The surrounding email thread provides the progression of discovery communications. Earlier in the thread, on February 13, 2023, Betsy Catron asked whether discovery could be set or a demand could be provided by the end of the month; Aaron Whaley replied that he did not object to the motion and would like to set mediation in April or May. On March 14, 2023, Betsy asked if a demand could be provided by month’s end, and Aaron responded, “I hope so.” By May 12, 2023, Betsy was again asking when a demand could be expected. On June 16, 2023, Aaron sent a message to Betsy and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1804], announcing that the demand had been added to a Dropbox folder, and provided a link to that folder.

On June 27, 2023, Aaron told Betsy that he would get the discovery to her as soon as possible and that he was agreeable to the opposing party’s response at mediation; he also noted that his schedule was favorable for mediation in late August or early September. Betsy replied that she had attached the discovery served on April 28, 2023 and inquired whether the parties were agreeable to a mediation timeline and dates with Judge Knopf; Aaron confirmed he would be available and reiterated willingness to proceed. Betsy later indicated that the firm was working on the responses, explaining that Amy had previously sent answers to Sarena while on bereavement leave and those responses had been mislaid; they had been found and were being drafted.

In late July 2023, Betsy asked when discovery responses could be expected. Aaron replied that the team was working on them and that the answers Amy had previously provided were located and would be drafted into the current set of responses. Betsy’s communications through July include the note that the discovery responses were anticipated and that a mediation timeline was being considered.

In August 2023, the thread shows further activity. On August 8, 2023, Aaron directed Bryce Koon to draft the discovery responses with blanks for any missing information. Bryce responded that he had in fact attempted contact with Amy—texting, emailing, and calling—with no response. Later that day and into August 9, 2023, the thread documents the ongoing process: Aaron’s August 9 email reiterates the phone conversation with Amy, confirms the lack of documents, records the eight-year gap since the most recent job, and notes the plan to supplement if new information becomes available. The August 9 note also includes the directive to finalize the filing and to add the caveat about Amy’s memory for certain details, such as phone numbers.

Throughout this period, the communications reflect the parties’ attempts to obtain complete discovery responses and to coordinate mediation timing. The thread references the assistant contacts, the disposition of discovery responses, the status of Amy’s information, and the use of a Dropbox folder to house the demand. The matter remains set toward resolution via mediation, with mediation discussions anticipated for late August to early September, and the overall objective of finalizing discovery responses despite the gaps in available information. Aaron Whaley’s and Betsy Catron’s signatures and contact details appear in the thread, along with the contact information for Betsy’s firm, Vine Center, located at 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, with a private phone number listed as [PHONE-1805] and a fax number of [PHONE-1806].

In summary, the case notes reflect ongoing discovery efforts regarding Amy Mills’ work history, the absence of documentation for the relevant jobs (the most recent being eight years prior), and a sequence of communications dating from February through August 2023 aimed at drafting discovery responses, securing missing information (including supervisor names and wage amounts), coordinating mediation, and organizing documents via a Dropbox link, all while acknowledging Amy’s limited memory for certain details and the plan to supplement as information becomes available.

item_394.note: In the case Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1818], the client is Amy Mills. He explained that he has been doing some work on her case and has a couple of questions and things he needs from her, if possible.

He noted that he had sent her an email with questions and requests for documents from the defense in her case and asked her to check her email and answer these questions as soon as possible so that he may send them over. He closed by thanking her and expressing the hope that he would hear from her soon.

item_396.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1823]. Client: Amy Mills.

In the matter titled Amy Mills, counsel communications show ongoing discovery and demand negotiations between Betsy R. Catron of Vine Center and Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm, with activity spanning from February through July 2023. The record begins with an exchange on February 13, 2023, when Aaron Whaley wrote to Betsy Catron that he did not object to her motion and that he would like to have mediation set in April or May.

On March 13, 2023, Betsy asked Aaron whether he thought he could get a demand by the end of the month. Aaron replied on March 14, 2023 with the brief, “I hope so.” The thread continued as Betsy followed up on May 12, 2023, asking, “When can I expect a demand from your client?” and stating that she had attached related materials.

Subsequently, on June 16, 2023, Aaron responded that he had added the demand to a Dropbox folder, providing a link to the folder for Betsy to access: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0. The next day, June 27, 2023, at 10:21 AM Betsy wrote back, thanking Aaron and requesting (a) the discovery responses served on 4/28/23, noting that she had attached them, (b) clarification about the 30-day response period for the demand and whether the parties could allow a response at mediation, and (c) Aaron’s availability for mediation in late August or early September, as she was arranging a mediation with Judge Knopf.

Aaron replied at 10:25 AM on June 27, 2023, agreeing to provide the discovery responses promptly, confirming that he was agreeable to a mediation-based response, and reiterating that his schedule was suitable for late August to early September.

On July 21, 2023, Betsy again asked Aaron when the discovery responses would be provided. Aaron’s reply, dated July 21, 2023 at 11:44 AM, stated that they were working on it. He explained that Amy had sent the answers to Sarena while Sarena was out on bereavement leave, and those responses had become lost in the shuffle; Aaron noted that they had found the responses and were in the process of drafting answers.

The thread shows that the matter involves the delivery of a discovery response for Amy Mills Premise and a demand related to the case, with mediation contemplated for late summer to early fall and ongoing coordination between the two law firms. The communications include direct references to specific dates, the Dropbox link containing the demand, and the noted issue of lost responses during a bereavement-related absence. The contact details in the correspondence identify Betsy Catron as the attorney for Vine Center in Lexington, KY, and Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm, with associated email addresses and firm information reflected in the exchanges.

item_397.note: This case file is titled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1825] and concerns client Amy Mills. On Friday, July 21, 2023, at 10:19:12 a.m. Eastern Time, Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager for The Whaley Law Firm at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222 (D: [PHONE-1826]; O: [PHONE-1827] ext 104; E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com; note that there is a new email address), forwarded to Bryce Koon an email with the subject “Fwd: Skills and training for WLF to send in a emial.” In the body of that message, she wrote, “Here is what she sent me back on June 2nd while I was out.”

The forwarded message itself contains the original email from Amy Mills. It was sent via Google Docs on Friday, June 2, 2023, at approximately 2:50 PM (time shown as 2:50?PM). Its subject line was “Skills and training for WLF to send in a emial,” and the To field is blank. Amy Mills attached a document and is shown with a profile image. The sender is listed as Amy Mills with the email address [EMAIL-1828], and she states that she has attached the following document. The body of Amy’s message includes the following text: “I am going to go ahead and email this to you with a DISCLAIMER Due to the nature of things this is done to the best of my known ability at this time with the information that was available. Please send back a note letting me know that you received and view this document thank you.” The message also includes the line “Skills and training for WLF to send in a emial.” The header notes Google LLC as the service provider, with the address Google LLC, 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA. It further states, “You have received this email because [EMAIL-1829] shared a document with you from Google Docs,” and displays a Google-related image.

item_399.note: Case: Amy Mills – Premise [DOB-1833]
Client: Amy Mills

In the Amy Mills matter, communications relating to discovery and mediation unfolded over several months in 2023. On July 21, 2023, Bryce Koon of Whaley Law Firm reported via email that he had sent Amy Mills the discovery questions but had been unable to reach her. He stated that he had called, texted, and emailed without receiving a response.

That same July 21 email included a forwarded message thread. Aaron Whaley’s initial entry in the thread, sent Friday, July 21, 2023 at 10:03 AM, was directed to Bryce Koon and Sarena Tuttle under the subject Fwd: Amy Mills, and it contained Betsy Catron’s inquiry about discovery responses. Betsy had previously sent on July 21, 2023 at 9:54 AM a message to Aaron Whaley asking, “Aaron, when can we expect responses to our discovery?” and noted that Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1834] had a corresponding file alias at [EMAIL-1835].

The thread shows earlier exchanges between Betsy Catron and Aaron Whaley. In a June 27, 2023 email, Aaron replied at 10:25 AM that he would get the discovery to Betsy asap and that he was agreeable to receiving a response at mediation, with his schedule open for late August to early September. Betsy had previously asked, in a June 27, 2023 email at 10:21 AM, for the discovery responses served on 4/28/23, attaching those documents. She also asked whether the 30-day response requirement could be satisfied by responding at mediation and inquired about availability for a mediation with Judge Knopf in late August or early September.

The correspondence also includes a June 16, 2023 email from Aaron to Betsy Catron and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1836], in which Aaron said he had added the demand to a Dropbox folder and provided the link: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0.

Earlier still, Betsy Catron had written on May 12, 2023, asking when a demand could be expected from Aaron’s client. Aaron’s response in this thread was not until June 16, 2023, when he provided the Dropbox link containing the demand.

The record also includes communications from February 13, 2023, with Aaron stating that he did not object to Betsy’s motion and that he would like to get mediation set in April or May, followed by repeated signatures for Aaron Whaley, whose firm is Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, with phone number [PHONE-1837] (the signature blocks also include the firm’s standard contact details). The chain shows a March 13, 2023 exchange in which Betsy asked if a demand could be provided by the end of the month; Aaron replied on March 14, 2023, “I hope so.” Betsy had previously asked on March 13, 2023 (2:59 PM) “Do you think you can get me a demand by the end of the month?”

The emails include standard external email caution footers indicating caution when opening attachments and links from external senders. The material also shows repeated signature blocks for Betsy Catron, Attorney at Vine Center, with the address 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, and contact details (P [PHONE-1838]; F [PHONE-1839]; www.whtlaw.com), and for Aaron Whaley at Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, with the same phone number.

In summary, the file records a sequence of discovery-related discussions and mediation planning spanning February through July 2023, culminating in July 21, 2023, when a Whaley Law Firm representative reported difficulty contacting Amy Mills and shared the prior discovery and discovery-related communications with the involved parties. The underlying file maintained the case reference Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1840] and the alias [EMAIL-1841] for the materials connected to the matter.

item_403.note: In the case titled Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1849], the client is Amy Mills. This note captures a request from the defense that followed a communications thread dated July 6, 2023.

On Thursday, July 6, 2023, at 10:20:21 a.m. Eastern Time, W. Bryce Koon, Esq., of The Whaley Law Firm (located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222) sent an electronic message to Ms. Mills at [EMAIL-1850] and CC’ed [EMAIL-1851]. The message begins with a greeting, “Good morning Ms. Mills,” and explains that there are a couple of questions from the defense for which the sender does not easily have the answers and is hoping she can help.

The defense’s questions request: (1) the names and addresses of every person or organization where Ms. Mills has worked or interned in the areas of Substance Abuse, Behavioral Disorder and Mental Health Counseling, or where she has worked as a LPCA (Licensed Professional Counselor Associate) or worked toward a LPC (Licensed Professional Counselor) designation; (2) for each person or organization identified, the dates of the employment, practicum, or internship; the nature of the work or duties; the places where such work or duties were performed; the names and telephone numbers of any supervisors for each employment, practicum, or internship; and the average weekly or monthly compensation from such employment, practicum, or internship; (3) copies of any applications for any such employment or internships above, including the date of the applications and the result of the application; and (4) any other documents that relate in any way to the above questions. The message closes with “Thank you!”

The sender signs as W. Bryce Koon, Esq., The Whaley Law Firm, and provides the firm’s contact information: 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; Phone: [PHONE-1852]; Fax: [PHONE-1853].

The email also contains a confidentiality disclaimer stating that the electronic message is intended only for the named recipient and may contain attorney-client privileged r otherwise protected information. If received in error, the recipient is instructed to contact The Whaley Law Firm and advise in this regard, with note that no waiver of any privilege shall be applicable, and that the recipient should not use the information contained herein or forward it to any third person not intended to receive it.

item_404.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1855]. Client: Amy Mills.

On June 27, 2023, W. Bryce Koon, Esq., of The Whaley Law Firm (located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; phone [PHONE-1856]; fax [PHONE-1857]) sent an email titled “RE: Amy Mills” to the distribution list for Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1858], including Aaron Whaley and Sarena Tuttle. The message, which carried the date Tue, Jun 27, 2023 at 10:27:36 AM (Eastern time zone offset -0400), stated, “I will work on them now.” The email also contained a standard confidentiality and privilege notice indicating that the electronic message is intended only for the named recipient and outlining steps to take if received in error, including contacting The Whaley Law Firm and not using or forwarding the information.

Earlier that same day, Aaron Whaley forwarded a message titled “Fwd: Amy Mills” to Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1859], Bryce Koon, and Sarena Tuttle with the note, “We need to respond to these requests.” This forwarded message included a prior thread beginning with Betsy Catron’s reply.

In Betsy Catron’s June 27, 2023 email (dated 10:21 AM), she acknowledged Aaron’s message and requested responses to the discovery served on 4/28/23, noting that the discovery was attached. She also asked whether a 30-day response could be accommodated by providing responses at mediation, and she inquired about dates for mediation with Judge Knopf, asking for availability in late August or early September. Betsy signed with her attorney information for Vine Center at 333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, providing the firm’s phone number [PHONE-1860]) and fax [PHONE-1861]) and listing the website www.whtlaw.com. A standard caution about external emails was included in the thread.

The thread also showed prior communications from Aaron Whaley. On June 16, 2023, at 3:26 PM, Aaron informed Betsy that he had added the demand to a Dropbox folder and provided a link to the folder: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0. This message followed Betsy’s earlier inquiries about the timing of the demand, including Betsy’s May 12, 2023 email asking, “When can I expect a demand from your client?” and Aaron’s March 14, 2023 reply, “I hope so,” in response to Betsy’s March 13, 2023 question: “Do you think you can get me a demand by the end of the month?” The preceding February 13, 2023 message from Aaron to Betsy indicated, “I don’t object to your motion. I would like to get mediation set in April or May,” followed by the signature blocks that included his contact information and a note about external email cautions.

In sum, the record documents ongoing discovery-related communications in the Amy Mills Premise case, including: a discovery response timeline being discussed (with a 30-day expectation), the plan to address those responses at mediation, the scheduling considerations with Judge Knopf for late summer or early fall, and the involvement of both Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1862] and counsel from The Whaley Law Firm in coordinating these efforts. The exchange includes multiple attachments and references to a Dropbox link for the demand, and the thread repeatedly notes the cautionary boilerplate about external emails.

item_405.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1864]. Client: Amy Mills.

This note is a forwarded email thread regarding the Amy Mills matter. The forward, labeled “__Fwd: Amy Mills__,” was sent on June 27, 2023 at 10:25:11 a.m. by Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm to the addresses Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1865], Bryce Koon, and Sarena Tuttle. The subject line was simply “RE: Amy Mills.” The message begins with, “We need to respond to these requests,” and then includes a chain of prior communications tied to the case.

The forwarded portion contains a message from Betsy Catron dated June 27, 2023 at 10:21 a.m. Betsy, identified as an attorney with Vine Center in Lexington, KY, wrote to Aaron Whaley and Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1866]. She thanked Aaron and asked that responses be provided to the discovery served on April 28, 2023, noting that she had attached those discovery requests. She also observed that the demand in the file carries a 30-day response period and asked if the responding party could address the demand at mediation. Betsy stated she was having her assistant check for mediation dates with Judge Knopf and inquired about Aaron’s availability for late August or early September.

The email thread includes earlier correspondence. On June 16, 2023, Aaron replied to Betsy and to Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1867], saying, “Betsy, here you go. Thank you for your patience. I have added the demand to a dropbox folder,” and provided the link: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0.

Prior to that, Betsy had written on May 12, 2023, asking, “When can I expect a demand from your client?” Aaron’s response at that time is not quoted in full, but the thread includes Betsy’s signature with her contact information for Vine Center—333 West Vine Street, Suite 1100, Lexington, KY 40507, phone [PHONE-1868], fax [PHONE-1869], and the website www.whtlaw.com.

Earlier still, on March 14, 2023, Aaron replied to Betsy with the message, “I hope so.” Betsy had asked on March 13, 2023, whether Aaron could obtain a demand by the end of the month. The portion of the thread beginning February 13, 2023 shows Aaron writing, “I don’t object to your motion. I would like to get mediation set in April or May—,” followed by Aaron’s full signature block for Whaley Law Firm, including the office address at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, and phone numbers. The emails repeatedly include standard external-email cautions in the signatures.

In summary, the file documents a back-and-forth from February through June 2023 regarding mediation timing and the production of a demand and discovery responses. By June 27, 2023, Betsy was requesting responses to discovery served on April 28, 2023, and to know whether the parties could handle the 30-day demand response at mediation, with plans to pursue mediation dates with Judge Knopf in late August or early September. The June 16, 2023 email confirms that the demand had been uploaded to a Dropbox folder for access.

item_406.note: In the Amy Mills Premise case (case file: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1871]), the client is Amy Mills. The record reflects a sequence of late winter to summer 2023 communications between counsel concerning discovery, a discovery demand, and mediation scheduling.

Beginning in February 2023, Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm indicated he did not object to Betsy Catron’s motion and expressed an interest in moving mediation forward, stating he would like to set mediation in April or May. On March 13, 2023, Betsy asked whether Aaron’s side could provide a demand by the end of the month; Aaron replied on March 14, 2023, that he “hop[e] so.”

On May 12, 2023, Betsy again inquired, asking, “When can I expect a demand from your client?” and she appears to have awaited a response. By June 16, 2023, Aaron responded, providing the demand and noting that he had added it to a Dropbox folder, with the message: “Betsy, here you go. Thank you for your patience. I have added the demand to a dropbox folder.” The link provided was https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0.

On June 27, 2023, Betsy wrote again, requesting responses to the discovery served on April 28, 2023, and asked whether the 30-day response period could be satisfied by responding at mediation. She also indicated she was coordinating a mediation with Judge Knopf and sought Aaron’s availability for late August or early September. Aaron replied on the same day, June 27, 2023, at 10:24:41 a.m. (-0400): “I will get the discovery to you asap. Yes I am agreeable to your response at mediation. My schedule is good late August early September.”

item_407.note: Case: Amy Mills (Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1873]) involves client Amy Mills. The note comprises forwarded emails between Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, and Melissa Wilder of Aptiva Health, concerning scheduling and costs for an Independent Medical Examination (IME) with Dr. Manderino.

In the forward dated June 26, 2023 at 11:07:17 AM Eastern Time, Sarena forwarded Dr. Manderino’s cost information for review. She stated that there is a charge of $600 per hour for record review on top of the $2,500 exam costs, and she asked for guidance on which records the two law firm recipients would like to have sent in order to minimize costs. The message was signed by Sarena M. Tuttle, who is a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her direct line is [PHONE-1874] and the office line is [PHONE-1875] ext 104. She also noted her new email address as Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, and provided the email address as a point of contact. The firm’s file also indicates the forward is addressed to Aaron Whaley ([EMAIL-1876]) and Bryce Koon ([EMAIL-1877]).

The forward also includes a previous message from Melissa Wilder. Dated Thursday, June 22, 2023 at about 1:54 PM, the message was addressed to Sarena@whaleylawfirm.com with the subject line “Amy Mills.” The content confirms that the firm had requested scheduling an IME for client Amy Mills with Dr. Manderino. It states that Dr. Manderino’s fee for an IME is $2,500, and there is also a surcharge billed at $600 per hour for the review of records exceeding 250 pages. The appointment is anticipated to take up to four hours. Dr. Manderino asked Melissa Wilder to send the attached disclaimer when scheduling IMEs, and she asked for an acknowledgment of receipt and to be informed whether the Whaley Law Firm would like to proceed.

The message from Melissa Wilder is signed as Administrative Specialist, with the following contact details: telephone number [PHONE-1878], extension 2669; email [EMAIL-1879]; website aptivahealth.com; and the mailing address 426 Codell Drive, Lexington, KY 40509. The signature also includes a standard confidentiality notice stating that the email and any attachments are intended only for the named addressees and may contain legally privileged or confidential information. It warns that dissemination, distribution, printing, or copying is strictly prohibited if the recipient is not the intended one, and instructs recipients who received the email in error to respond to the sender and permanently delete the original and any copies.

item_408.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1882]. Client: Amy Mills.

On June 22, 2023, Melissa Wilder, Administrative Specialist at Aptiva Health, sent an email from [EMAIL-1883] to Sarena Whaley (Sarena@whaleylawfirm.com) regarding scheduling an independent medical examination (IME) for Amy Mills with Dr. Manderino. Wilder conveyed that Dr. Manderino’s IME fee is $2,500, and there is an additional surcharge billed at $600 per hour for the review of records exceeding 250 pages. The appointment is expected to take up to four hours. Dr. Manderino asked Wilder to send the attached disclaimer when scheduling IMEs. Wilder requested that Sarena acknowledge receipt and indicate whether they would like to proceed.

Thank you,

Melissa Wilder
Administrative Specialist
[PHONE-1884], ext. 2669
[EMAIL-1885]
aptivahealth.com
426 Codell Drive
Lexington, KY 40509

This e-mail, and any attachments thereto, is intended only for the addressee(s) named herein and may contain legally privileged and/or confidential information. If you are not the intended recipient of this e-mail (or the person responsible for delivering this document to the intended recipient), you are hereby notified that any dissemination, distribution, printing or copying of this e-mail, and any attachment thereto, is strictly prohibited. If you have received this e-mail in error, please respond to the individual sending the message, and permanently delete the original and any copy of any e-mail and printout thereof.

item_416.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1905]. Client: Amy Mills. Re: Amy Mills. This note records a sequence of email communications between Betsy Catron of Vine Center/WHT Law and Aaron Whaley of Whaley Law Firm regarding Amy Mills Premise, with the file dated June 16, 2023.

The thread begins with a February 13, 2023 email from Aaron Whaley to Betsy Catron. In that message, he writes, “I don’t object to your motion. I would like to get mediation set in April or May--,” and his signature block identifies him as with Whaley Law Firm, located at 712 Lyndon Ln, Louisville, KY 40222, with the phone number [PHONE-1906]. The email also contains the standard external-email caution notice and his full signature.

On March 13, 2023, Betsy Catron asked, “Do you think you can get me a demand by the end of the month?” Aaron Whaley replied the following day, March 14, 2023, with a brief, “I hope so.” The exchange continued on May 12, 2023, when Betsy again inquired, “When can I expect a demand from your client?” The thread includes Betsy’s attorney contact details for Vine Center, Lexington, KY (address and phone as listed in the note).

Finally, on June 16, 2023, Aaron Whaley sent a message titled “Re: Amy Mills,” stating, “Betsy, here you go. Thank you for your patience. I have added the demand to a Dropbox folder,” and he provided a link to the folder: https://www.dropbox.com/sh/0imoh5irinhb1bb/AACZwtaR1soOYXeuy9ox1F4ta?dl=0. This June 16, 2023 email is directed to Betsy Catron and to Amy Mills Premise in the email header, and is time-stamped Fri, 16 Jun 2023 15:25:53 -0400. The message formats and signature blocks reflect the standard attorney communications content included in the record.

item_419.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1912]. Client: Amy Mills.

In the matter regarding Amy Mills, the correspondence centers on arranging an Independent Medical Examination (IME) and a medical narrative with Dr. Lisa Manderino at Aptiva to determine whether Amy’s headaches are related to a fall she sustained. This plan was first proposed by Aaron Whaley on June 14, 2023 at approximately 3:47 PM, who wrote: “Lets set up Amy for an IME and medical narrative with Dr. Lisa Manderino at Aptiva. This will be to see if Amy's headaches are related to her fall.”

The thread continued on June 15, 2023. At about 2:39 PM (time shown as 2:39?PM in the original), Sarena M. Tuttle, a Litigation Paralegal and Workers’ Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm (712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222), replied: “I will get this scheduled.” Her signature identifies her role and contact information: D: [PHONE-1913]; O: [PHONE-1914] ext 104; E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com; note that this is a new email address. The message also notes that she sent a contact email to Aptiva for scheduling and that she is awaiting a response.

The email thread includes the following details: the original message was sent on June 14, 2023; the June 15 reply from Sarena confirms scheduling efforts; and the thread shows that Amy Mills Premise [DOB-1915] was CC’d on the correspondence. The requested action remains: to schedule an IME and medical narrative with Dr. Lisa Manderino at Aptiva to assess whether Amy’s headaches are related to her fall.

The correspondence is dated June 15, 2023, at 14:50:50 -0400, under the Re: Amy Mills subject, with the initial case reference and client clearly identified.

item_423.note: Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1934]. Client: Amy Mills.

The earliest entry in this correspondence is an email from Amy Mills sent on June 5, 2023 at 7:09?AM. In that message she wrote to Sarena, starting with “Hey Serena,” and asking, “Just wanted to make sure you received my email on Friday. Thank you.” Amy signed the message with her name, and included the closing line: “The heart of a fool is in his mouth, but the mouth of a wise man is in his heart.” — Benjamin Franklin.

The next page of the thread shows a reply from Sarena M. Tuttle on Monday, June 12, 2023 at 3:36?PM. In that message she apologized for the delayed response, explaining that her father had recently passed away and she had been out of the office. She confirmed that she did receive Amy’s skills email, had saved the document to Amy’s file, and had forwarded a copy to Aaron for his review as well. The signature block identifies Sarena as a Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with the firm’s address at 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222. Her contact details are listed as D: [PHONE-1935], O: [PHONE-1936] ext 104, and E: Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note stating “PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.”

The following entry is from Amy Mills on Wednesday, June 14, 2023 at 12:28:38 -0400. In this message Amy writes: “Good Afternoon, I am so sorry to hear that. My condolences dear. Thank you for the update. Take the time you need, I of all people know how hard it is to make it through daily life with everything that it throws at you. Thank you again, Amy Mills.” The message is sent from Amy Mills at [EMAIL-1937] to Sarena Tuttle at sarena@whaleylawfirm.com. The chain also includes Sarena’s earlier note about forwarding the document to Aaron for review and acknowledges the work on Amy’s file.

item_426.note: This case note concerns Amy Mills (Case: Amy-Mills-Premise-[DOB-1949]) with the client identified as Amy Mills. On Friday, June 2, 2023, at 18:50:16 UTC, an email was sent from Amy Mills, listed as “Amy Mills (via Google Docs)” using the address [EMAIL-1950], to sarena@whaleylawfirm.com, with no CC recipients included. The message included an attachment titled “Skills and training for WLF to send in a emial.pdf” and was sent via Google Docs, as indicated by the line “Sent using Google Docs” and the link https://docs.google.com/.

The body of the message indicates that the sender intended to email this document with a disclaimer. The exact content reads: “I am going to go ahead and email this to you with a DISCLAIMER Due to the nature of things this is done to the best of my known ability at this time with the information that was available. Please send back a note letting me know that you received and view this document thank you.” The sender requested a return note confirming that the recipient received and viewed the document.

item_427.note: Case: Amy Mills — Premise [DOB-1952]. Client: Amy Mills.

In the email thread titled “Re: Your Personal Injury Claim - New Request for Information,” the communications relate to Amy Mills’s personal injury claim and a new request for information from the defense. On June 1, 2023, at 4:56 p.m. Eastern Time, Amy Mills sent a message to Sarena Tuttle. In that note, she explained that she had been dealing with numerous issues, including difficulty locating her materials in storage. She also stated that she had just undergone an unexpected root canal procedure. She said she would send whatever documentation she could within the next 24 hours and expressed a hope that nothing had been left out, emphasizing that she did not want to do anything wrong. She closed with a quick update and thanks, signing as Amy Mills.

Earlier in the thread, on May 4, 2023, at 10:54 a.m., Sarena Tuttle replied to Amy. Sarena explained that nothing was attached to the prior communication. She acknowledged that the circumstances were difficult and that the impact of the incident extended beyond the physical injury to include mental, emotional, financial, and spiritual effects. She emphasized that the goal was not to make things more difficult, and that if information could be obtained by other means they would pursue those options. She noted that the defense was currently requesting Amy’s employment history and that the matter could not move forward until that information was received, while recognizing that gathering it might take a few days. Sarena proposed breaking down the request to start with a list of places where Amy had provided counseling or social work services, with the intention of gradually gathering the more detailed information the defense sought. She reassured Amy that the firm was there to help and that they were willing to do whatever was needed to assist her. The message included Sarena’s contact details: Sarena M. Tuttle, Litigation Paralegal & Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, 712 Lyndon Lane, Louisville, KY 40222; direct line [PHONE-1953]; office line [PHONE-1954] ext. 104; email Sarena@WhaleyLawFirm.com, with a note to “PLEASE NOTE NEW EMAIL ADDRESS.”

In that same day’s correspondence, at 9:07 a.m., Amy Mills wrote describing the daily challenges she faced. She detailed that she had spent hours on a document due to concentration difficulties, the need to manage sit/stand rotation, trouble typing, and visual disturbances that caused words to blur and separate on the page. She mentioned having to write a school excuse for her child and being unable to hold a pen because of numbness, sharp electric "zaps," and muscle spasms, all related to the accident. She described the frustration of encountering unspoken struggles that were not visible at a casual glance and the ongoing difficulty of obtaining consistent medical diagnoses from different doctors who often relied only on scans and test results without her input. She stated that she would send the completed document and that someone would help her put it together as soon as possible, noting that this had become a daily reality she continued to manage. She signed off with thanks and again stated her intention to send the completed document soon.

Earlier on May 3, 2023, at 3:11 p.m., Sarena replied to Amy with appreciation, saying, “Awesome, thank you so much,” and adding, “Again, I apologize for the frustration and confusion.” The signature block identified Sarena M. Tuttle as Litigation Paralegal and Workers' Compensation Case Manager at The Whaley Law Firm, with the same Louisville address and contact details as above.

Moments later, at 2:35 p.m. on May 3, Amy Mills said she would list the items and send them for review, inviting Sarena to advise if anything should be added or changed before proceeding.

At 1:06 p.m. on May 3, Sarena sent a direct request to Amy: she needed a list of Amy’s jobs that fell under the counseling/social worker umbrella, including any internships or paid employment. She confirmed that both she and another colleague, Aaron, had reviewed the file and communications to Cecilia, and asked Amy simply to provide a list of all roles under that umbrella.

Earlier still, at 12:44 p.m. on May 3, Amy Mills vented frustration about having sent all requested information multiple times. She explained that emails related to her case had been forwarded to The Whaley Law Firm (WLF) because her case had changed case managers several times, but the documentation had not been forwarded consistently. She questioned whether she was understood correctly. She expressed that compiling and submitting documentation was an extremely difficult task given the time that had passed and the circumstances affecting the case. She discussed potential confusion surrounding whether items would be accepted and suggested that emails containing documentation should be filed for present or future use. She speculated about mixups involving titles such as cosmetologist/beautician, social worker, and certified counselor/therapist, along with training and education, and indicated that she had attempted to provide screen shots as proof of time/date stamps, even before a deposit, to support the information. She stated she was seeking saved emails to re-forward the documentation and remarked that she had little control over how her information was stored or transferred within her case.

The chain ends with Amy’s partially complete note, cut off mid-sentence: “I have no control over how my information in stored, transferred, or otherwise for my case within.” 

In summary, the record shows Amy Mills communicating on June 1, 2023, to provide an update and promise to send available materials within 24 hours, following an earlier exchange in early May where the defense’s information requests prompted detailed, repeated attempts by Amy to supply employment and counseling/social work history, a substantial explanation of the personal and health-related difficulties she was experiencing, and ongoing coordination with Sarena Tuttle of The Whaley Law Firm regarding how best to gather and organize the information.
